declarations.name: load_cached_extraction
declarations.doc_comment:
Loads a cached extraction result from disk.
Returns `null` if the cache is missing, corrupted, or has a version mismatch.
This makes the cache self-healing: any error triggers re-extraction.

parameters.name: cache_path
parameters.description: absolute path to the cache file

declarations.name: save_cached_extraction
declarations.doc_comment:
Saves an extraction result to the cache.
Uses atomic write (temp file + rename) for crash safety.
Normalizes empty collections to null to avoid allocation overhead on load.
declarations.type_signature: (deps: CacheDeps, cache_path: string, content_hash: string, extraction: ExtractionData): Promise<void>

parameters.name: cache_path
parameters.description: absolute path to the cache file

parameters.name: content_hash
parameters.description: SHA-256 hash of the source file contents

declarations.name: delete_cached_extraction
declarations.doc_comment:
Deletes a cached extraction file.
Silently succeeds if the file doesn't exist.

parameters.name: cache_path
parameters.description: absolute path to the cache file

declarations.name: from_cached_extraction
declarations.doc_comment:
Converts a cached extraction back to the runtime format.
Preserves null semantics (null = empty).

modules.module_comment:
Cache infrastructure for incremental CSS class extraction.

Provides per-file caching with content hash validation to avoid
re-extracting classes from unchanged files.

declarations.name: css_class_definitions
declarations.doc_comment: All built-in CSS class definitions (token classes + composites).

declarations.name: merge_class_definitions
declarations.doc_comment:
Merges user class definitions with the built-in defaults.
User definitions take precedence over defaults with the same name.

throws.description: if `include_defaults` is false and no user definitions provided

declarations.type_signature: (user_definitions: Record<string, CssClassDefinition | undefined> | undefined, include_defaults: boolean): Record<string, CssClassDefinition | undefined>
declarations.return_description: merged class definitions

modules.module_comment:
Built-in CSS class definitions combining token classes and composites.

Token classes map to CSS variables (design tokens).
For other CSS properties, use CSS-literal syntax like `display:flex` or `position:absolute`.

declarations.name: ExtractionData
declarations.doc_comment:
Cacheable extraction data fields shared between extraction and caching layers.
Uses `null` instead of empty collections to avoid allocation overhead.

properties.name: classes
properties.doc_comment:
Map from class name to locations where it was used, or null if none.
Keys = unique classes, values = locations for diagnostics/IDE integration.

properties.name: explicit_classes
properties.doc_comment:
Classes explicitly annotated via `@fuz-classes` comments, or null if none.
These produce errors if they can't be resolved during generation.

properties.name: diagnostics
properties.doc_comment: Diagnostics from the extraction phase, or null if none

properties.name: elements
properties.doc_comment:
HTML elements found in the file, or null if none.
Used for including only the `style.css` rules needed.

properties.name: explicit_elements
properties.doc_comment:
Elements explicitly annotated via `@fuz-elements` comments, or null if none.
These should produce errors if they have no matching style rules.

properties.name: explicit_variables
properties.doc_comment:
Variables explicitly annotated via `@fuz-variables` comments, or null if none.
These produce errors if they can't be resolved to theme variables.

declarations.name: has_extraction_data
declarations.doc_comment: Returns true if the extraction data has any non-null fields.

declarations.name: ExtractionResult
declarations.doc_comment:
Extraction result with classes mapped to their source locations.
Extends `ExtractionData` with `tracked_vars` which is internal to AST walking.

Uses embedded diagnostics (rather than a Result type) because file extraction
can partially succeed: some classes may be extracted while others produce errors.
This differs from `CssLiteralParseResult` which uses a discriminated union
because single-class parsing is binary success/failure.

properties.name: tracked_vars
properties.doc_comment: Variables that were used in class contexts, or null if none

declarations.name: AcornPlugin
declarations.doc_comment: Acorn plugin type - a function that extends the Parser class.

declarations.name: ExtractCssClassesOptions
declarations.doc_comment: Options for CSS class extraction.

properties.name: filename
properties.doc_comment:
File path used to determine extraction method (Svelte vs TS)
and for location tracking in diagnostics.

properties.name: acorn_plugins
properties.doc_comment:
Additional acorn plugins to use when parsing TS/JS files.
Useful for adding JSX support via `acorn-jsx` for React projects.

declarations.name: SourceIndex
declarations.doc_comment:
Helper class for converting character offsets to line/column positions.
Svelte template nodes (Comment, Text, ExpressionTag) only have char offsets,
so this class enables efficient conversion.

Build: O(n) where n = source length
Lookup: O(log m) where m = number of lines (binary search)

members.name: get_location
members.doc_comment: Converts a character offset to a source location.

declarations.name: extract_from_svelte
declarations.doc_comment: Extracts CSS classes from a Svelte file using AST parsing.
declarations.return_description: extraction result with classes, tracked variables, elements, and diagnostics

declarations.name: extract_from_ts
declarations.doc_comment: Extracts CSS classes from a TypeScript/JS file using AST parsing.
declarations.type_signature: (source: string, file?: string, acorn_plugins?: AcornPlugin[] | undefined): ExtractionResult
declarations.return_description: extraction result with classes, tracked variables, elements, and diagnostics

parameters.name: acorn_plugins
parameters.description: additional acorn plugins (e.g., acorn-jsx for React)

declarations.name: extract_css_classes
declarations.doc_comment:
Unified extraction function that auto-detects file type.
Returns just the class names as a Set.
declarations.return_description: set of class names, or null if none found

declarations.name: extract_css_classes_with_locations
declarations.doc_comment:
Unified extraction function that auto-detects file type.
Returns full extraction result with locations and diagnostics.
declarations.return_description: full extraction result with classes, tracked variables, and diagnostics

modules.module_comment:
AST-based CSS class extraction for Svelte and TypeScript files.

Replaces regex-based extraction with proper parsing to handle:
- `class="display:flex"` - string attributes
- `class={{ active, disabled: !enabled }}` - object attributes (Svelte 5.16+)
- `class={[cond && 'box', 'display:flex']}` - array attributes (Svelte 5.16+)
- `class:active={cond}` - class directives
- `clsx('foo', { bar: true })` - class utility function calls
- Variables with class-related names
- `// @fuz-classes class1 class2` - comment hints for dynamic classes
- `// @fuz-elements element1 element2` - comment hints for dynamic elements
- `// @fuz-variables var1 var2` - comment hints for dynamic CSS variables

declarations.name: escape_css_selector
declarations.doc_comment:
Escapes special characters in a CSS class name for use in a selector.
CSS selectors require escaping of characters like `:`, `%`, `(`, `)`, etc.
examples:
```ts
escape_css_selector('display:flex') // 'display\\:flex'
escape_css_selector('opacity:80%') // 'opacity\\:80\\%'
escape_css_selector('nth-child(2n)') // 'nth-child\\(2n\\)'
```

declarations.name: extract_primary_property
declarations.doc_comment:
Extracts the CSS property name from a single-property declaration string.
Returns `null` for multi-property declarations (composites with multiple semicolons).
examples:
```ts
extract_primary_property('border-radius: var(--border_radius_sm);') // 'border-radius'
extract_primary_property('display: flex; align-items: center;') // null
```

declarations.name: CssClassDefinitionComposition
declarations.doc_comment: Pure utility composition (composes only).

declarations.name: CssClassDefinitionDeclaration
declarations.doc_comment: Custom CSS declaration (optionally seeded with composes).

declarations.name: CssClassDefinitionRuleset
declarations.doc_comment: Full ruleset with selectors.

declarations.name: CssClassDefinitionStatic
declarations.doc_comment: Static definitions (not interpreter-based).

declarations.name: CssClassDefinition
declarations.doc_comment: Full union including interpreters.

declarations.name: CssClassInterpreterContext
declarations.doc_comment:
Context passed to CSS class interpreters.
Provides access to logging, diagnostics collection, and the class registry.

properties.name: log
properties.doc_comment: Optional logger for warnings/errors

properties.name: diagnostics
properties.doc_comment: Diagnostics array to collect warnings and errors

properties.name: class_definitions
properties.doc_comment: All known CSS class definitions (token + composite classes)

properties.name: css_properties
properties.doc_comment: Valid CSS properties for literal validation, or null to skip validation

declarations.name: CssClassDefinitionInterpreter
declarations.doc_comment: Interpreter for dynamic CSS class generation based on pattern matching.

declarations.name: GenerateClassesCssResult
declarations.doc_comment: Result from CSS class generation.

properties.name: variables_used
properties.doc_comment: CSS variables used by the generated classes (without -- prefix)

properties.name: css_properties
properties.doc_comment: Valid CSS properties for literal validation, or null to skip validation

properties.name: explicit_classes
properties.doc_comment:
Classes that were explicitly annotated (via `@fuz-classes` or `additional_classes`).
Unresolved explicit classes produce warnings.

modules.module_comment:
CSS class generation utilities.

Produces CSS output from class definitions, handles interpretation of
dynamic classes, and provides collection management for extracted classes.

declarations.name: generate_classes
declarations.doc_comment:
Generates CSS class declarations from templates.
Supports up to 3 dimensions of multiplicative combinations.
examples:
```ts
// Simple list
generate_classes(
  v => ({ name: `position_${v}`, css: `position: ${v};` }),
  ['static', 'relative', 'absolute']
)
```

```ts
// Two dimensions (multiplicative)
generate_classes(
  (dir, size) => ({ name: `m${dir}_${size}`, css: `margin-${dir}: ${size};` }),
  ['top', 'bottom'],
  ['0', '1px', '2px']
)
```
declarations.type_signature: <T1 = string, T2 = string, T3 = string>(template: ClassTemplateFn<T1, T2, T3>, values: Iterable<T1>, secondary?: Iterable<T2> | undefined, tertiary?: Iterable<...> | undefined): Record<...>

parameters.name: template
parameters.description: function that generates CSS from values, can return null to skip

parameters.name: tertiary
parameters.description: optional third dimension for even more combinations

declarations.name: format_spacing_value
declarations.doc_comment:
Format spacing values for CSS (handles 0, auto, percentages, pixels, and CSS variables).
Used by margin, padding, gap, inset, top/right/bottom/left, etc.

declarations.name: format_dimension_value
declarations.doc_comment:
Format width/height values for CSS (handles 0, auto, percentages, pixels, content values, and CSS variables).
Used by width and height properties.

declarations.name: generate_property_classes
declarations.doc_comment: Generate classes for a single CSS property with various values.
declarations.type_signature: (property: string, values: Iterable<string>, formatter?: ((value: string) => string) | undefined, prefix?: string): Record<string, CssClassDefinition>

parameters.name: values
parameters.description: the values to generate classes for

parameters.name: formatter
parameters.description: optional function to format values (e.g. v => `var(--space_${v})`)

parameters.name: prefix
parameters.description: optional class name prefix (defaults to property with dashes replaced by underscores)

declarations.name: generate_directional_classes
declarations.doc_comment:
Generate directional classes for properties like margin and padding.
Creates classes for all directions: base, top, right, bottom, left, x (horizontal), y (vertical).
declarations.type_signature: (property: string, values: Iterable<string>, formatter?: ((v: string) => string) | undefined): Record<string, CssClassDefinition>

parameters.name: values
parameters.description: the values to generate classes for

parameters.name: formatter
parameters.description: optional function to format values (defaults to identity)

declarations.name: generate_border_radius_corners
declarations.doc_comment:
Generate border radius corner classes for all four corners.
Creates classes for top-left, top-right, bottom-left, bottom-right corners.
declarations.type_signature: (values: Iterable<string>, formatter?: ((value: string) => string) | undefined): Record<string, CssClassDefinition>

parameters.name: values
parameters.description: the values to generate classes for

declarations.name: generate_shadow_classes
declarations.doc_comment:
Generate shadow classes for various shadow types and sizes.
Creates classes for regular, top, bottom, inset, inset-top, and inset-bottom shadows.
Each shadow uses color-mix with alpha values for transparency.
declarations.type_signature: (sizes: Iterable<string>, alpha_mapping: Record<string, string>): Record<string, CssClassDefinition>

parameters.name: sizes
parameters.description: the shadow size variants (xs, sm, md, lg, xl)

parameters.name: alpha_mapping
parameters.description: mapping of sizes to alpha numbers (1-5)

declarations.name: modified_class_interpreter
declarations.doc_comment:
Interpreter for modified token/composite classes (e.g., `hover:p_md`, `md:box`, `dark:hover:panel`).
Applies modifiers to existing declaration-based or ruleset-based classes.

This interpreter must run BEFORE `css_literal_interpreter` to handle cases like `hover:box`
where `box` is a known class (not a CSS property).

declarations.name: css_literal_interpreter
declarations.doc_comment:
Interpreter for CSS-literal classes (e.g., `display:flex`, `hover:opacity:80%`).
Generates full CSS rulesets including any modifier wrappers.

declarations.name: css_class_interpreters
declarations.doc_comment:
Collection of all builtin interpreters for dynamic CSS class generation.
Order matters: `modified_class_interpreter` runs first to handle `hover:box` before
`css_literal_interpreter` tries to interpret it as `hover:box` (property:value).

declarations.name: ResolveComposesResult
declarations.doc_comment: Result from resolving a `composes` array to combined declarations.

declarations.name: resolve_class_definition
declarations.doc_comment:
Resolves a class definition's declaration, handling `composes` composition.

If the definition has a `composes` property, resolves those classes recursively
and combines with any explicit `declaration`. If no `composes`, returns the
explicit `declaration` directly.
declarations.type_signature: (def: CssClassDefinitionStatic, class_name: string, definitions: Record<string, CssClassDefinition | undefined>, css_properties?: Set<...> | null): ResolveComposesResult
declarations.return_description: combined declaration or an error

parameters.name: class_name
parameters.description: the name of the class being resolved (for error messages)

parameters.name: definitions
parameters.description: record of all known class definitions

parameters.name: css_properties
parameters.description: set of valid CSS properties for literal validation, or null to skip

declarations.name: resolve_composes
declarations.doc_comment:
Resolves an array of class names to their combined CSS declarations.

Recursively resolves nested `composes` arrays and combines all declarations.
Validates that referenced classes exist and are resolvable (not rulesets or interpreters).
Supports unmodified CSS literals (e.g., `text-align:center`) in the composes array.

Deduplication behavior:
- Diamond dependencies (class reached via different composition branches) are silently skipped
- Redundant listings (class already included by an earlier sibling in this array) emit a warning
declarations.type_signature: (class_names: string[], definitions: Record<string, CssClassDefinition | undefined>, resolution_stack: Set<string>, visited: Set<string>, original_class_name: string, css_properties?: Set<...> | null): ResolveComposesResult
declarations.return_description: combined declarations or an error

parameters.name: class_names
parameters.description: array of class names to resolve

parameters.name: definitions
parameters.description: record of all known class definitions

parameters.name: resolution_stack
parameters.description: set of class names currently being resolved (for cycle detection)

parameters.name: visited
parameters.description: set of all class names already resolved (for deduplication)

parameters.name: original_class_name
parameters.description: the class name being defined (for error messages)

parameters.name: css_properties
parameters.description: set of valid CSS properties for literal validation, or null to skip

modules.module_comment:
CSS class resolution utilities for composing class definitions.

Provides the `resolve_composes` helper to recursively resolve class names
to their combined CSS declarations.

declarations.name: CssClasses
declarations.doc_comment:
Collection of CSS classes extracted from source files.
Tracks classes per-file for efficient incremental updates.
Handles include/exclude filtering and explicit class tracking.
Uses `null` instead of empty collections to avoid allocation overhead.

members.name: constructor
members.doc_comment: Creates a new `CssClasses` collection.
members.type_signature: (additional_classes?: Set<string> | null, exclude_classes?: Set<string> | null): CssClasses

parameters.name: additional_classes
parameters.description: classes to always include (also treated as explicit for warnings)

parameters.name: exclude_classes
parameters.description: classes to exclude from output (also suppresses warnings)

members.name: add
members.doc_comment:
Adds extraction results for a file.
Replaces any previous classes and diagnostics for this file.

parameters.name: data
parameters.description: extraction data fields to store for this file

members.name: get
members.doc_comment: Gets all unique class names as a Set (with exclude filter applied).

members.name: get_with_locations
members.doc_comment:
Gets all classes with their source locations (with exclude filter applied).
Locations from `additional_classes` are null.

members.name: get_all
members.doc_comment:
Gets all classes and their locations in a single call.
More efficient than calling `get()` and `get_with_locations()` separately
when both are needed (avoids potential double recalculation).

Results have exclude filter applied and `explicit_classes` includes `additional_classes`.
members.type_signature: (): { all_classes: Set<string>; all_classes_with_locations: Map<string, SourceLocation[] | null>; explicit_classes: Set<string> | null; all_elements: Set<...>; explicit_elements: Set<...> | null; explicit_variables: Set<...> | null; }
members.return_type: { all_classes: Set<string>; all_classes_with_locations: Map<string, SourceLocation[] | null>; explicit_classes: Set<string> | null; all_elements: Set<...>; explicit_elements: Set<...> | null; explicit_variables: Set<...> | null; }

members.name: get_diagnostics
members.doc_comment: Gets all extraction diagnostics from all files.

modules.module_comment:
Collection management for extracted CSS classes.

Tracks classes per-file for efficient incremental updates during watch mode.
Uses `null` instead of empty collections to avoid allocation overhead.

declarations.name: ParsedCssLiteral
declarations.doc_comment: Parsed CSS-literal class with all components extracted.

properties.name: class_name
properties.doc_comment: Original class name

properties.name: media
properties.doc_comment: Media modifier (breakpoint or feature query)

properties.name: ancestor
properties.doc_comment: Ancestor modifier (dark/light)

properties.name: states
properties.doc_comment: State modifiers in alphabetical order (can have multiple)

properties.name: pseudo_element
properties.doc_comment: Pseudo-element modifier (before, after, etc.)

properties.name: property
properties.doc_comment: CSS property name

properties.name: value
properties.doc_comment: CSS value (with ~ replaced by spaces)

declarations.name: CssLiteralParseResult
declarations.doc_comment:
Result of parsing a CSS-literal class name.

Uses a discriminated union (Result type) because parsing a single class
is binary: it either succeeds or fails entirely. This differs from
`ExtractionResult` which uses embedded diagnostics because file
extraction can partially succeed (some classes extracted, others have errors).

Uses `| null` for diagnostics to avoid allocating empty arrays.
Callers should use a guard pattern: `if (result.diagnostics) { ... }`

declarations.name: ExtractedModifiers
declarations.doc_comment:
Extracted modifiers from a class name.
Used by both CSS-literal parsing and modified class interpretation.

properties.name: media
properties.doc_comment: Media modifier (breakpoint or feature query)

properties.name: ancestor
properties.doc_comment: Ancestor modifier (dark/light)

properties.name: states
properties.doc_comment: State modifiers in alphabetical order (can have multiple)

properties.name: pseudo_element
properties.doc_comment: Pseudo-element modifier (before, after, etc.)

declarations.name: ModifierExtractionResult
declarations.doc_comment: Result of extracting modifiers from segments.

declarations.name: InterpretCssLiteralResult
declarations.doc_comment:
Result of interpreting a CSS-literal class.

Uses `| null` for warnings to avoid allocating empty arrays.
Callers should use a guard pattern: `if (result.warnings) { ... }`

declarations.name: load_css_properties
declarations.doc_comment:
Loads CSS properties from `@webref/css`.
Returns a fresh Set each time - callers should cache the result if needed.

declarations.name: is_valid_css_property
declarations.doc_comment:
Checks if a property name is a valid CSS property.
Custom properties (--*) always return true.
declarations.return_description: true if valid CSS property or custom property

parameters.name: property
parameters.description: the CSS property name to validate

parameters.name: properties
parameters.description:
Set of valid CSS properties from `load_css_properties()`
  Pass `null` to skip validation.

declarations.name: suggest_css_property
declarations.doc_comment: Suggests a correct property name for a typo using Levenshtein distance.
declarations.type_signature: (typo: string, properties: Set<string> | null): string | null
declarations.return_description: the suggested property or null if no close match (Levenshtein distance > 2)

parameters.name: properties
parameters.description:
Set of valid CSS properties from `load_css_properties()`
  Pass `null` to skip suggestions.

declarations.name: suggest_modifier
declarations.doc_comment: Suggests a correct modifier name for a typo using Levenshtein distance.
declarations.return_description: the suggested modifier or null if no close match (Levenshtein distance > 2)

declarations.name: format_css_value
declarations.doc_comment:
Formats a CSS-literal value for CSS output.
- Replaces `~` with space
- Ensures space before `!important`
declarations.return_description: formatted CSS value

declarations.name: check_calc_expression
declarations.doc_comment: Checks if a value contains a possibly invalid calc expression.
declarations.return_description: warning message if suspicious, null otherwise

declarations.name: is_possible_css_literal
declarations.doc_comment:
Checks if a class name could be a CSS-literal class.
Quick check before attempting full parse.
declarations.return_description: true if it could be CSS-literal syntax

declarations.name: extract_segments
declarations.doc_comment:
Extracts colon-separated segments from a class name, handling parentheses.
Parenthesized content (like function arguments) is kept intact.
examples:
```ts
extract_segments('md:hover:display:flex') // → ['md', 'hover', 'display', 'flex']
extract_segments('nth-child(2n+1):color:red') // → ['nth-child(2n+1)', 'color', 'red']
extract_segments('width:calc(100%-20px)') // → ['width', 'calc(100%-20px)']
```

declarations.name: extract_and_validate_modifiers
declarations.doc_comment:
Extracts and validates modifiers from the beginning of a segments array.
Modifiers are consumed from the front until a non-modifier segment is found.

Used by both CSS-literal parsing and modified class interpretation.
declarations.return_description: `ModifierExtractionResult` with modifiers and remaining segments, or error

parameters.name: class_name
parameters.description: original class name for error messages

declarations.name: parse_css_literal
declarations.doc_comment: Parses a CSS-literal class name into its components.
declarations.return_description: `CssLiteralParseResult` with parsed data or error

parameters.name: css_properties
parameters.description:
Set of valid CSS properties from `load_css_properties()`
  Pass `null` to skip property validation.

declarations.name: generate_selector
declarations.doc_comment:
Generates the CSS selector for a parsed CSS-literal class.
Includes state pseudo-classes and pseudo-element in the selector.

declarations.name: generate_declaration
declarations.doc_comment: Generates the CSS declaration for a parsed CSS-literal class.

declarations.name: CssLiteralOutput
declarations.doc_comment: Information needed to generate CSS output for a CSS-literal class.

properties.name: declaration
properties.doc_comment: CSS declaration (property: value;)

properties.name: selector
properties.doc_comment: Full CSS selector including pseudo-classes/elements

properties.name: media_wrapper
properties.doc_comment: Media query wrapper if any

properties.name: ancestor_wrapper
properties.doc_comment: Ancestor wrapper if any

declarations.name: interpret_css_literal
declarations.doc_comment:
Interprets a CSS-literal class and returns CSS generation info.

Callers should first check `is_possible_css_literal()` to filter non-CSS-literal classes.
declarations.type_signature: (class_name: string, escaped_class_name: string, css_properties: Set<string> | null): InterpretCssLiteralResult
declarations.return_description: result with output and warnings on success, or error on failure

parameters.name: escaped_class_name
parameters.description: the CSS-escaped version of the class name

parameters.name: css_properties
parameters.description:
Set of valid CSS properties from `load_css_properties()`
  Pass `null` to skip property validation.

declarations.name: LiteralResolutionResult
declarations.doc_comment: Result of attempting to resolve a CSS literal for composition.

declarations.name: has_modifiers
declarations.doc_comment: Checks if a parsed CSS literal has any modifiers.

declarations.name: has_extracted_modifiers
declarations.doc_comment:
Checks if extracted modifiers contain any modifier.
Useful for tooling that needs to detect modified classes.

declarations.name: try_resolve_literal
declarations.doc_comment:
Attempts to resolve a class name as an unmodified CSS literal for composition.

Used by `resolve_composes` to support literals in `composes` arrays.
Returns the declaration if successful, null if not a literal, or an error
if it's a literal with issues (modifiers, invalid property, etc.).
declarations.type_signature: (class_name: string, css_properties: Set<string> | null, context_class_name: string): LiteralResolutionResult
declarations.return_description: resolution result with declaration, or error, or null if not a literal

parameters.name: class_name
parameters.description: the class name to try resolving

parameters.name: css_properties
parameters.description: set of valid CSS properties, or null to skip validation

parameters.name: context_class_name
parameters.description: the class being defined (for error messages)

declarations.name: generate_css_literal_simple
declarations.doc_comment:
Generates simple CSS for a CSS-literal class (without grouping).
Used by the interpreter for basic output.
declarations.return_description: CSS string for this class

modules.module_comment:
CSS-literal syntax parser, validator, and interpreter.

Enables writing utility classes using actual CSS syntax:

- `display:flex` → `.display\:flex { display: flex; }`
- `hover:opacity:80%` → `.hover\:opacity\:80\%:hover { opacity: 80%; }`
- `md:dark:hover:before:opacity:80%` → nested CSS with media query, ancestor, state, pseudo-element

@see {@link https://github.com/fuzdev/fuz_css} for documentation

declarations.name: CssExtractionOptions
declarations.doc_comment:
Options for CSS class extraction from source files.
Controls which files to scan and how to parse them.

properties.name: filter_file
properties.doc_comment:
Filter function to determine which files to extract classes from.
By default, extracts from .svelte, .html, .ts, .js, .tsx, .jsx files,
excluding test files and .gen files.

properties.name: acorn_plugins
properties.doc_comment:
Additional acorn plugins for parsing.
Use `acorn-jsx` for React/Preact/Solid projects.

declarations.name: CssClassOptions
declarations.doc_comment:
Options for CSS class definitions and interpretation.
Controls how classes are defined and how dynamic classes are generated.

properties.name: class_definitions
properties.doc_comment:
Additional class definitions to merge with defaults.
User definitions take precedence over defaults with the same name.
Required when `include_default_classes` is `false`.

properties.name: include_default_classes
properties.doc_comment:
Whether to include default class definitions (token and composite classes).
When `false`, `class_definitions` is required.

properties.name: class_interpreters
properties.doc_comment:
Custom interpreters for dynamic class generation.
Replaces the builtin interpreters entirely if provided.

declarations.name: BaseCssOption
declarations.doc_comment:
Type for the base_css option used by CSS generators.

Supports four forms:
- `undefined` - Use default `style.css` (framework defaults)
- `null` - Disable base styles entirely (explicit opt-out)
- `string` - Custom CSS to replace defaults
- `(default_css) => string` - Callback to modify default CSS

See module documentation for the `undefined` vs `null` convention.

declarations.name: VariablesOption
declarations.doc_comment:
Type for the variables option used by CSS generators.

Supports four forms:
- `undefined` - Use default variables (framework defaults)
- `null` - Disable theme generation entirely (explicit opt-out)
- `Array<StyleVariable>` - Custom variables array (replaces defaults)
- `(defaults) => Array<StyleVariable>` - Callback to modify defaults

See module documentation for the `undefined` vs `null` convention.

declarations.name: CssOutputOptions
declarations.doc_comment:
Options for CSS output generation (theme + base + utilities).
Controls how the three CSS layers are combined.

properties.name: base_css
properties.doc_comment:
Base styles (element defaults) configuration.
- `undefined` (default): Use default style.css
- `null`: Disable base styles entirely
- `string`: Custom CSS to replace defaults
- `(default_css) => string`: Callback to modify default CSS

properties.name: variables
properties.doc_comment:
Theme variables configuration.
- `undefined` (default): Use default variables from fuz_css
- `null`: Disable theme entirely
- `Array<StyleVariable>`: Custom variable definitions (replaces defaults)
- `(defaults) => Array<StyleVariable>`: Callback to modify default variables

properties.name: theme_specificity
properties.doc_comment:
Specificity multiplier for theme CSS selectors.
Defaults to 1 which generates `:root`, higher values generate more specific selectors (e.g., `:root:root`).

properties.name: additional_classes
properties.doc_comment:
Classes to always include in the output, regardless of detection.
Useful for dynamically generated class names that can't be statically extracted.

properties.name: additional_elements
properties.doc_comment:
Additional HTML elements to always include base styles for.
Use `'all'` to include all base styles regardless of detection.
Useful for elements generated at runtime via `document.createElement()`.

properties.name: additional_variables
properties.doc_comment:
Additional CSS variables to always include in theme output.
Use `'all'` to include all theme variables regardless of detection.
Useful for variables referenced dynamically.

properties.name: exclude_classes
properties.doc_comment:
Classes to exclude from the output, even if detected.
Useful for filtering out false positives from extraction.

properties.name: exclude_elements
properties.doc_comment:
Elements to exclude from base CSS output, even if detected.
Useful for filtering out elements you don't want styles for.

properties.name: exclude_variables
properties.doc_comment:
CSS variables to exclude from theme output, even if referenced.
Useful for filtering out variables you don't want in the theme.

declarations.name: CssDiagnosticsOptions
declarations.doc_comment: Options for error and warning handling.

properties.name: on_error
properties.doc_comment:
How to handle CSS-literal errors during generation.
- 'log': Log errors, skip invalid classes, continue
- 'throw': Throw on first error, fail the build

properties.name: on_warning
properties.doc_comment:
How to handle warnings during generation.
- 'log': Log warnings, continue
- 'throw': Throw on first warning, fail the build
- 'ignore': Suppress warnings entirely

declarations.name: CssCacheOptions
declarations.doc_comment: Options for cache behavior.

properties.name: cache_dir
properties.doc_comment: Cache directory relative to project root.

properties.name: deps
properties.doc_comment:
Filesystem deps for cache management.
Defaults to production implementation. Override for testing.

declarations.name: CssGeneratorBaseOptions
declarations.doc_comment:
Combined base options shared by both Gro and Vite plugins.
These options work identically in both contexts.

modules.module_comment:
Shared options for CSS generation plugins (Gro and Vite).

Both `gen_fuz_css` (Gro generator) and `vite_plugin_fuz_css` share
the same core options for extraction, generation, and bundled CSS.
This module provides the shared types to ensure consistency.

## Bundled mode (default)

By default, the generated CSS (`virtual:fuz.css` or `./fuz.css`) includes
only the content your code uses from all three layers:
- Base styles (element defaults)
- Theme variables (CSS custom properties)
- Utility classes

## `undefined` vs `null` Convention

Configuration options that accept both `undefined` and `null` follow this pattern:

- **`undefined`** - Use framework defaults. The feature is enabled with standard behavior.
- **`null`** - Explicitly disable the feature. No output is generated for that layer.

This applies to `BaseCssOption` and `VariablesOption`.
Setting both to `null` enables "utility-only mode" where you manage
your own theme and base styles via direct imports (`@fuzdev/fuz_css/style.css`
and `theme.css`, which include all content).

declarations.name: ParsedRule
declarations.doc_comment: A parsed CSS rule with its components and positions.

properties.name: selector
properties.doc_comment: Full selector string (e.g., ".box", ".selectable:hover")

properties.name: selector_start
properties.doc_comment: Start position of selector in original CSS (0-indexed)

properties.name: selector_end
properties.doc_comment: End position of selector in original CSS

properties.name: declarations
properties.doc_comment: The declarations block (without braces)

properties.name: rule_start
properties.doc_comment: Start position of the entire rule

properties.name: rule_end
properties.doc_comment: End position of the entire rule

declarations.name: ParsedRuleset
declarations.doc_comment: Result of parsing a CSS ruleset.

properties.name: rules
properties.doc_comment: All rules in the ruleset

declarations.name: parse_ruleset
declarations.doc_comment: Parses a CSS ruleset string using Svelte's CSS parser.
declarations.return_description: `ParsedRuleset` with structured rule data and positions

declarations.name: is_single_selector_ruleset
declarations.doc_comment:
Checks if a ruleset has only a single simple selector (just the class name).
Used to detect rulesets that could be converted to declaration format.
declarations.return_description: true if there's exactly one rule with selector ".class_name"

declarations.name: ruleset_contains_class
declarations.doc_comment:
Checks if any selector in the ruleset contains the expected class name.
Used to validate that ruleset definitions match their key.
declarations.return_description: true if at least one selector contains ".class_name"

declarations.name: extract_css_comment
declarations.doc_comment:
Extracts the CSS comment from a ruleset (if any).
Looks for comments before the first rule.
declarations.return_description: comment text without delimiters, or null if no comment

declarations.name: SkippedModifierInfo
declarations.doc_comment:
Information about a modifier that was skipped for a selector during ruleset modification.
The selector is still included in output, just without the conflicting modifier applied.

properties.name: selector
properties.doc_comment: The specific selector where the modifier was skipped (not the full selector list)

properties.name: reason
properties.doc_comment: Reason the modifier was skipped

properties.name: conflicting_modifier
properties.doc_comment: The conflicting modifier that was not applied (e.g., "::before" or ":hover")

declarations.name: ModifiedSelectorGroupResult
declarations.doc_comment:
Result from modifying a selector group with conflict detection.

Uses `| null` for skipped_modifiers to avoid allocating empty arrays.
Callers should use a guard pattern: `if (result.skipped_modifiers) { ... }`

properties.name: selector
properties.doc_comment: The modified selector list as a string

properties.name: skipped_modifiers
properties.doc_comment: Information about modifiers skipped for specific selectors, or null if none

declarations.name: ModifiedRulesetResult
declarations.doc_comment:
Result from generating a modified ruleset.

Uses `| null` for skipped_modifiers to avoid allocating empty arrays.
Callers should use a guard pattern: `if (result.skipped_modifiers) { ... }`

properties.name: css
properties.doc_comment: The generated CSS

properties.name: skipped_modifiers
properties.doc_comment: Information about modifiers that were skipped for certain rules, or null if none

declarations.name: split_selector_list
declarations.doc_comment: Splits a selector list by commas, respecting parentheses, brackets, and quoted strings.
examples:
```ts
split_selector_list('.a, .b') // → ['.a', '.b']
split_selector_list('.a:not(.b), .c') // → ['.a:not(.b)', '.c']
split_selector_list('.a[data-x="a,b"], .c') // → ['.a[data-x="a,b"]', '.c']
```

declarations.name: find_compound_end
declarations.doc_comment:
Finds the end position of the compound selector containing the class at class_pos.
A compound selector is a sequence of simple selectors without combinators.
declarations.return_description: position where state modifiers should be inserted (before any pseudo-element)

declarations.name: modify_single_selector
declarations.doc_comment: Modifies a single CSS selector to add modifiers.
declarations.type_signature: (selector: string, original_class: string, new_class_escaped: string, state_css: string, pseudo_element_css: string): string
declarations.return_description: modified selector

parameters.name: selector
parameters.description: a single CSS selector (not a selector list)

declarations.name: modify_selector_group
declarations.doc_comment:
Modifies a selector list (comma-separated selectors) to add modifiers.
Handles conflicts per-selector: if one selector in a list has a conflict,
only that selector skips the modifier; other selectors still get it.
declarations.type_signature: (selector_group: string, original_class: string, new_class_escaped: string, states_to_add: string[], pseudo_element_css: string): ModifiedSelectorGroupResult
declarations.return_description: result with modified selector list and information about skipped modifiers

declarations.name: generate_modified_ruleset
declarations.doc_comment:
Generates CSS for a modified ruleset with applied modifiers.

Conflict handling is per-selector within selector lists:
- For `.plain:hover, .plain:active` with `hover:` modifier, only `.plain:hover` skips
  the `:hover` addition; `.plain:active` still gets `:hover` appended.
- For multiple states like `:hover:focus`, each is checked individually; conflicting
  states are skipped while non-conflicting ones are still applied.
declarations.type_signature: (original_ruleset: string, original_class: string, new_class_escaped: string, state_css: string, pseudo_element_css: string, media_wrapper: string | null, ancestor_wrapper: string | null): ModifiedRulesetResult
declarations.return_description: result with generated CSS and information about skipped modifiers

parameters.name: new_class_escaped
parameters.description: the escaped new class name with modifiers

modules.module_comment:
CSS ruleset parser using Svelte's CSS parser.

Parses CSS rulesets to extract selectors and declarations with position information.
Used for:
- Phase 0a: Detecting single-selector rulesets that could be converted to declaration format
- Phase 2: Modifying selectors for modifier support on composite classes

declarations.name: extract_css_variables
declarations.doc_comment:
Extracts CSS variable names from a CSS string.

Parses `var(--name)` patterns and returns the variable names
without the `--` prefix. Handles fallback values by extracting
only the primary variable reference.
declarations.return_description: set of variable names (without `--` prefix)

declarations.name: has_css_variables
declarations.doc_comment:
Checks if a CSS string contains any CSS variable references.

More efficient than `extract_css_variables` when you only need
to know if variables exist, not what they are.
declarations.return_description: true if the string contains `var(--*)` patterns

modules.module_comment:
CSS variable extraction utilities.

Provides shared helper functions for extracting CSS custom property references
from CSS strings. Used by style_rule_parser, variable_graph, class_variable_index,
and css_class_generation.

declarations.name: default_cache_deps
declarations.doc_comment: Default filesystem deps using `node:fs/promises`.

modules.module_comment:
Production implementations of deps interfaces.

Provides real filesystem deps for production use.
For interface definitions and dependency injection pattern, see `deps.ts`.

declarations.name: CacheDeps
declarations.doc_comment:
Cache-related file system deps.
Abstracted to enable test isolation from the actual filesystem.

Named `CacheDeps` (not `FsDeps`) because it only covers
the specific deps needed for cache management, not general
filesystem access.

properties.name: read_text

Reads a text file.

properties.name: write_text_atomic
properties.doc_comment:
Writes a text file atomically (temp file + rename for crash safety).
Creates parent directories if they don't exist.

properties.name: unlink
properties.doc_comment:
Removes a file. Returns a `not_found` error if the file doesn't exist —
callers that want `rm -f` semantics should ignore that kind explicitly.

declarations.name: SourceLocation
declarations.doc_comment: Source location for IDE/LSP integration.

properties.name: line
properties.doc_comment: 1-based line number

properties.name: column
properties.doc_comment: 1-based column number

declarations.name: BaseDiagnostic
declarations.doc_comment: Base diagnostic with common fields.

declarations.name: ExtractionDiagnostic
declarations.doc_comment: Diagnostic from the extraction phase.

declarations.name: GenerationDiagnostic
declarations.doc_comment: Diagnostic from the generation phase.

properties.name: identifier
properties.doc_comment: The class name, element name, or variable name this diagnostic refers to

properties.name: locations
properties.doc_comment: Source locations where this class was used, or null if from `additional_classes`

declarations.name: Diagnostic

declarations.name: InterpreterDiagnostic

Diagnostic from CSS class interpretation.
Used internally by interpreters; converted to `GenerationDiagnostic` with locations.

declarations.name: create_generation_diagnostic

declarations.name: format_diagnostic

declarations.name: CssGenerationError
declarations.doc_comment:
Error thrown when CSS generation encounters errors or warnings

Contains the full diagnostics array for programmatic access.

modules.module_comment:
Diagnostic types for CSS class extraction and generation.

Provides a unified diagnostic system across all phases:
- Extraction: Parsing source files to find class names
- Generation: Producing CSS output from class definitions

declarations.name: demoClass
declarations.doc_comment: `*Class` suffix (camelCase)

declarations.name: demo_class
declarations.doc_comment: `*_class` suffix (snake_case)

declarations.name: DEMO_CLASS
declarations.doc_comment: `SCREAMING_SNAKE_CASE` naming

declarations.name: demoClasses
declarations.doc_comment: `*Classes` suffix (camelCase) - plural, multiple classes

declarations.name: demo_classes
declarations.doc_comment: `*_classes` suffix (snake_case) - plural, multiple classes

declarations.name: demoClassName
declarations.doc_comment: `*ClassName` suffix (camelCase)

declarations.name: demo_class_name
declarations.doc_comment: `*class_name` suffix (snake_case)

declarations.name: demoClassNames
declarations.doc_comment: `*ClassNames` suffix (camelCase) - plural, multiple classes

declarations.name: demo_class_names
declarations.doc_comment: `*class_names` suffix (snake_case) - plural, multiple classes

declarations.name: demoClassList
declarations.doc_comment: `*ClassList` suffix (camelCase)

declarations.doc_comment: `*class_list` suffix (snake_case)

declarations.name: ternaryClass
declarations.doc_comment: Ternary expression - both branches extracted

declarations.name: logicalClass
declarations.doc_comment: Logical AND - truthy value extracted

declarations.name: arrayClasses
declarations.doc_comment: Array - all elements extracted

declarations.name: objectClasses
declarations.doc_comment: Object - keys extracted as class names

declarations.name: fromComment
declarations.doc_comment:
Comment hint - extracted via `@fuz-classes`, NOT via variable name pattern.
Variable name `fromComment` doesn't end in class/classes/className.

modules.module_comment:
Example CSS class exports demonstrating node_modules extraction.

This module exists to verify that the Vite plugin extracts classes from
dependencies in node_modules. The exports test specific extraction patterns
that require being in a separate module.

**Important:** Variable names must match `CLASS_NAME_PATTERN` in `css_class_extractor.ts`.
Supported suffixes: `class`, `classes`, `className`, `classNames`, `classList`, `classLists`
(also snake_case variants like `class_name`, `class_names`, `class_list`, `class_lists`).

**Patterns demonstrated:**
- Naming patterns: All `CLASS_NAME_PATTERN` suffix variants
- Expression patterns: Ternary, logical AND, arrays
- Comment hints: `@fuz-classes` directive

Token, composite, and literal classes are demonstrated inline in the examples
since they don't require special extraction testing.

declarations.name: FileFilter
declarations.doc_comment: Filter function to determine which files to process for CSS class extraction.

declarations.name: filter_file_default
declarations.doc_comment:
Default file filter for CSS class extraction.
Includes .svelte, .html, .ts, .js, .tsx, .jsx files.
Excludes test files (.test.ts, .spec.ts) and generated files (.gen.ts).
Excludes files in test directories (`/test/`, `/tests/`, `/__tests__/`, `/__mocks__/`).

modules.module_comment: File filtering utilities for CSS class extraction.

declarations.name: GenFuzCssOptions
declarations.doc_comment:
Options for the Gro CSS generator.
Extends the shared base options with Gro-specific settings.

properties.name: include_stats
properties.doc_comment: Whether to include file and resolution statistics in the output.

properties.name: project_root
properties.doc_comment: Project root directory. Source paths must be under this directory.

properties.name: concurrency
properties.doc_comment:
Max concurrent file processing (cache read + extract).
Bottlenecked by CPU-bound AST parsing.

properties.name: cache_io_concurrency
properties.doc_comment: Max concurrent cache writes and deletes (I/O-bound).

modules.module_comment:
[Gro generator](https://github.com/fuzdev/gro) for creating optimized utility CSS from extracted class names.
Scans source files, extracts CSS classes with AST-based parsing, and generates
only the CSS for classes actually used. Includes per-file caching with content
hash validation for fast incremental rebuilds.

declarations.name: ModifierType
declarations.doc_comment:
Type of modifier determining its position in the class name and CSS output.

Order in class names: `[media:][ancestor:][state...:][pseudo-element:]property:value`

declarations.name: ModifierDefinition
declarations.doc_comment: Definition for a single modifier.

properties.name: name
properties.doc_comment: The prefix used in class names (e.g., 'hover', 'md', 'dark')

properties.name: type
properties.doc_comment: Type determines position in modifier order and CSS output behavior

properties.name: css
properties.doc_comment: The CSS output - wrapper for media/ancestor, suffix for state/pseudo-element

properties.name: order
properties.doc_comment: Optional ordering within type (for breakpoints, sorted by this value)

declarations.name: MODIFIERS
declarations.doc_comment:
All modifier definitions in a single declarative structure.
Adding a new modifier requires only adding to this array.

declarations.name: MEDIA_MODIFIERS
declarations.doc_comment: Map of media modifier names to their CSS output

declarations.name: ANCESTOR_MODIFIERS
declarations.doc_comment: Map of ancestor modifier names to their CSS output

declarations.name: STATE_MODIFIERS
declarations.doc_comment: Map of state modifier names to their CSS output

declarations.name: PSEUDO_ELEMENT_MODIFIERS
declarations.doc_comment: Map of pseudo-element modifier names to their CSS output

declarations.name: ALL_MODIFIER_NAMES
declarations.doc_comment: All modifier names for quick lookup

declarations.name: NTH_CHILD_PATTERN
declarations.doc_comment: Pattern for parameterized nth-child: `nth-child(2n+1):`

declarations.name: NTH_LAST_CHILD_PATTERN
declarations.doc_comment: Pattern for parameterized nth-last-child: `nth-last-child(2n+1):`

declarations.name: NTH_OF_TYPE_PATTERN
declarations.doc_comment: Pattern for parameterized nth-of-type: `nth-of-type(2n):`

declarations.name: NTH_LAST_OF_TYPE_PATTERN
declarations.doc_comment: Pattern for parameterized nth-last-of-type: `nth-last-of-type(2n):`

declarations.name: extract_balanced_parens
declarations.doc_comment:
Extracts content from balanced parentheses after a prefix.
Supports nested parens for calc(), clamp(), min(), max(), etc.
examples:
```ts
extract_balanced_parens("min-width(800px)", "min-width") // "800px"
extract_balanced_parens("min-width(calc(100vw - 20px))", "min-width") // "calc(100vw - 20px)"
extract_balanced_parens("min-width(calc(100vw)", "min-width") // null (unbalanced)
```
declarations.return_description: the content inside the balanced parens, or null if no match/unbalanced

parameters.name: prefix
parameters.description: the prefix before the opening paren (e.g., "min-width")

declarations.name: parse_arbitrary_breakpoint
declarations.doc_comment:
Parses an arbitrary breakpoint modifier.
Supports nested parentheses for calc(), clamp(), min(), max(), var().
Requires value to start with a digit or known CSS function to catch obvious mistakes.
examples:
```ts
parse_arbitrary_breakpoint("min-width(800px)") // "@media (width >= 800px)"
parse_arbitrary_breakpoint("min-width(calc(100vw - 200px))") // "@media (width >= calc(100vw - 200px))"
parse_arbitrary_breakpoint("min-width(px)") // null (invalid - no digit or function)
```
declarations.return_description: the CSS media query or null if not an arbitrary breakpoint

declarations.name: parse_parameterized_state
declarations.doc_comment: Parses a parameterized state modifier (nth-child, nth-last-child, nth-of-type, nth-last-of-type).
declarations.type_signature: (segment: string): { name: string; css: string; type: "state"; } | null
declarations.return_type: { name: string; css: string; type: "state"; } | null
declarations.return_description: object with name (including parameter) and CSS, or null if not parameterized

declarations.name: get_modifier
declarations.doc_comment:
Gets the modifier definition for a segment.
Handles both static modifiers and dynamic patterns (arbitrary breakpoints, parameterized states).
declarations.return_description: the modifier definition or null if not a known modifier

declarations.name: get_all_modifier_names
declarations.doc_comment: Gets all modifier names for error message suggestions.

modules.module_comment:
Declarative modifier definitions for CSS-literal syntax.

Modifiers enable responsive, state-based, and contextual styling:
- Media modifiers: `md:`, `print:`, `motion-safe:`
- Ancestor modifiers: `dark:`, `light:`
- State modifiers: `hover:`, `focus:`, `disabled:`
- Pseudo-element modifiers: `before:`, `after:`

@see {@link https://github.com/fuzdev/fuz_css} for documentation

declarations.name: StyleRuleBase
declarations.doc_comment: Base fields shared by all style rules.

properties.name: css
properties.doc_comment: The full CSS text for this rule (including selector and declarations)

properties.name: elements
properties.doc_comment: HTML element names this rule targets (e.g., 'button', 'input')

properties.name: classes
properties.doc_comment: CSS class names this rule targets (e.g., 'unstyled', 'selected')

properties.name: variables_used
properties.doc_comment: CSS variables referenced in declarations

properties.name: order

declarations.name: CoreReason

declarations.name: CoreStyleRule
declarations.doc_comment: A core style rule that is always included in output.

declarations.name: NonCoreStyleRule
declarations.doc_comment: A non-core style rule included only when its elements/classes are detected.

declarations.name: StyleRule
declarations.doc_comment:
A parsed style rule with metadata for filtering.
Discriminated union: check `is_core` to narrow to `CoreStyleRule` or `NonCoreStyleRule`.
All rules have a consistent shape - `core_reason` is null for non-core rules.

declarations.name: StyleRuleIndex
declarations.doc_comment: Index of parsed style rules for efficient lookup.

properties.name: by_element

properties.name: by_class
properties.doc_comment: Rules indexed by class name

properties.name: content_hash

declarations.name: parse_style_css
declarations.doc_comment: Parses a CSS stylesheet into a `StyleRuleIndex`.
declarations.return_description: `StyleRuleIndex` with rules and lookup maps

declarations.name: load_style_rule_index

declarations.name: create_style_rule_index

Creates a `StyleRuleIndex` from a custom CSS string.

declarations.name: resolve_base_css_option
declarations.doc_comment:
Resolves a `base_css` option to a `StyleRuleIndex`.
Handles all option forms: undefined (defaults), null (disabled), string, or callback.
declarations.return_description: promise resolving to `StyleRuleIndex`, or null if disabled

parameters.name: deps

declarations.name: get_matching_rules
declarations.doc_comment: Gets rules that should be included based on detected elements and classes.
declarations.type_signature: (index: StyleRuleIndex, detected_elements: Set<string>, detected_classes: Set<string>): Set<number>
declarations.return_description: set of rule indices to include

parameters.name: detected_classes

declarations.name: generate_base_css

modules.module_comment:
Style.css rule parser for bundled CSS generation.

Parses the fuz_css `style.css` file into a structured index that maps
CSS rules to the HTML elements and classes they style. This enables
the bundled CSS generator to include only the rules needed for
elements actually used in the project.

properties.name: specificity
properties.doc_comment:
Repeats the theme selector to handle unpredictable head content insertion order.
Accepts any integer >= 1, defaults to 2.

declarations.name: render_theme_variable
declarations.type_signature: (variable: { name: string; light?: string | undefined; dark?: string | undefined; summary?: string | undefined; }, dark?: boolean, comments?: boolean): string

parameters.name: variable
parameters.type: { name: string; light?: string | undefined; dark?: string | undefined; summary?: string | undefined; }

declarations.name: default_themes
declarations.doc_comment: These are super basic proof-of-concept themes.

declarations.name: NumericScaleVariant
declarations.doc_comment:
The standard numeric scale used across multiple variable families.
Provides 13 steps from 00 (surface/subtle) to 100 (contrast/bold).

declarations.name: NumericScaleWithExtremesVariant
declarations.doc_comment:
Numeric scale with min/max extremes for scales that need untinted endpoints.
Used by text and shade scales where min/max represent pure black/white values.

declarations.name: TextScaleVariant
declarations.doc_comment: Full text scale including min/max extremes for knockout text.

declarations.name: ShadeScaleVariant
declarations.doc_comment: Full shade scale including min/max extremes for untinted surfaces.

declarations.name: icon_sizes
declarations.type_signature: { icon_size_xs: string; icon_size_sm: string; icon_size_md: string; icon_size_lg: string; icon_size_xl: string; icon_size_xl2: string; icon_size_xl3: string; }

declarations.name: Z_INDEX_MAX
declarations.doc_comment: Maximum value for CSS z-index property (32-bit signed integer max).

declarations.name: StyleVariableInfo
declarations.doc_comment: Information about a single style variable and its dependencies.

properties.name: name
properties.doc_comment: Variable name (without -- prefix)

properties.name: light_deps
properties.doc_comment: Variables referenced in the light value

properties.name: dark_deps
properties.doc_comment: Variables referenced in the dark value

properties.name: light_css
properties.doc_comment: The CSS value for light mode, or undefined if not defined

properties.name: dark_css
properties.doc_comment: The CSS value for dark mode, or undefined if not defined

declarations.name: VariableDependencyGraph
declarations.doc_comment: Dependency graph for style variables.

properties.name: variables
properties.doc_comment: Map from variable name to its info

properties.name: content_hash
properties.doc_comment: Content hash for cache invalidation

declarations.name: build_variable_graph
declarations.doc_comment: Builds a dependency graph from an array of style variables.
declarations.type_signature: (variables: { name: string; light?: string | undefined; dark?: string | undefined; summary?: string | undefined; }[], content_hash: string): VariableDependencyGraph
declarations.return_description: `VariableDependencyGraph`

parameters.name: variables
parameters.type: { name: string; light?: string | undefined; dark?: string | undefined; summary?: string | undefined; }[]

parameters.name: content_hash
parameters.description: hash of the source for cache invalidation

declarations.name: ResolveVariablesResult
declarations.doc_comment: Result from transitive variable resolution.

properties.name: variables
properties.doc_comment: All resolved variable names

properties.name: warnings
properties.doc_comment: Warning messages for cycles

properties.name: missing
properties.doc_comment: Variable names that were requested but not found in the graph

declarations.name: resolve_variables_transitive
declarations.doc_comment:
Resolves variables transitively using DFS with cycle detection.
When a variable is requested, all variables it depends on are included.
Both light and dark dependencies are always resolved together.
declarations.return_description: `ResolveVariablesResult` with all transitive dependencies

parameters.name: initial_variables
parameters.description: initial set of variable names to resolve

declarations.name: generate_theme_css
declarations.doc_comment: Generates theme CSS for the resolved variables.
declarations.type_signature: (graph: VariableDependencyGraph, resolved_variables: Set<string>, specificity?: number): { light_css: string; dark_css: string; }
declarations.return_description: object with `light_css` and `dark_css` strings

parameters.name: resolved_variables
parameters.description: set of variable names to include

parameters.name: specificity
parameters.description: number of times to repeat the selector (default 1)

declarations.name: get_all_variable_names
declarations.doc_comment: Gets all variable names from the graph.
declarations.return_description: set of all variable names in the graph

declarations.name: has_variable
declarations.doc_comment: Checks if a variable exists in the graph.
declarations.return_description: true if the variable exists in the graph

declarations.name: find_similar_variable
declarations.doc_comment:
Finds the most similar variable in the graph to the given name.
Returns null if no variable exceeds the similarity threshold.
declarations.return_description: the most similar variable name, or null if none are similar enough

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: the variable name to find similar matches for

declarations.name: resolve_variables_option
declarations.doc_comment: Resolves a variables option to a concrete array of style variables.
declarations.type_signature: (variables: VariablesOption): { name: string; light?: string | undefined; dark?: string | undefined; summary?: string | undefined; }[]
declarations.return_type: { name: string; light?: string | undefined; dark?: string | undefined; summary?: string | undefined; }[]
declarations.return_description: resolved array of style variables, or empty array if null

parameters.name: variables
parameters.description: the variables option (undefined, null, array, or callback)

declarations.name: build_variable_graph_from_options
declarations.doc_comment:
Builds a variable dependency graph from a variables option.
Handles all option forms: undefined (defaults), null (disabled), array, or callback.
declarations.return_description: `VariableDependencyGraph` built from the resolved variables

parameters.name: variables
parameters.description: the variables option from generator config

modules.module_comment:
Variable dependency graph for bundled CSS generation.

Builds a dependency graph from style variables to enable transitive
resolution of CSS custom properties. When a variable is needed,
all variables it depends on are also included.

declarations.name: StyleVariable
declarations.doc_comment:
Zod schema for validating `StyleVariable` objects.
Use `safeParse` for validation; the `StyleVariable` type is defined separately
to preserve the `Flavored` brand on `name`.
declarations.type_signature: ZodObject<{ name: ZodString; light: ZodOptional<ZodString>; dark: ZodOptional<ZodString>; summary: ZodOptional<ZodString>; }, $strip>

declarations.name: DiagnosticSeverity
declarations.doc_comment:
Diagnostic severity levels.

- `error`: Analysis failed, declaration may be incomplete or missing data
- `warning`: Partial success, something seems off but analysis continued

declarations.name: DiagnosticKind
declarations.doc_comment: Discriminant for diagnostic types.

declarations.name: BaseDiagnostic
declarations.doc_comment: Base diagnostic fields shared by all diagnostic types.

properties.name: file
properties.doc_comment: File path relative to project root (display with './' prefix).

properties.name: line
properties.doc_comment: Line number (1-based), or null if location unavailable.

properties.name: column
properties.doc_comment: Column number (1-based), or null if location unavailable.

properties.name: message
properties.doc_comment: Human-readable description of the issue.

declarations.name: TypeExtractionDiagnostic
declarations.doc_comment: Type extraction failed (e.g., complex or recursive types).

properties.name: symbol_name
properties.doc_comment: Name of the symbol whose type couldn't be extracted.

declarations.name: SignatureAnalysisDiagnostic
declarations.doc_comment: Function/method signature analysis failed.

properties.name: function_name
properties.doc_comment: Name of the function or method.

declarations.name: ClassMemberDiagnostic
declarations.doc_comment: Class member analysis failed.

properties.name: class_name
properties.doc_comment: Name of the class.

properties.name: member_name
properties.doc_comment: Name of the member that failed.

declarations.name: SveltePropDiagnostic
declarations.doc_comment: Svelte prop type resolution failed.

properties.name: component_name
properties.doc_comment: Name of the component.

properties.name: prop_name
properties.doc_comment: Name of the prop.

declarations.name: ModuleSkippedDiagnostic
declarations.doc_comment:
Module was skipped during analysis.
Could be due to missing source file in program or no analyzer available.

properties.name: reason
properties.doc_comment: Reason the module was skipped.

declarations.name: Diagnostic
declarations.doc_comment: Union of all diagnostic types.

declarations.name: AnalysisContext
declarations.doc_comment:
Context for collecting diagnostics during source analysis.

Thread an instance through analysis functions to collect errors and warnings
without halting analysis. After analysis completes, check `has_errors()` and
report collected diagnostics.

members.name: add
members.doc_comment: Add a `Diagnostic` to the collection.

members.name: has_errors
members.doc_comment: Check if any errors were collected.

members.name: has_warnings
members.doc_comment: Check if any warnings were collected.

members.name: errors
members.doc_comment: Get all error diagnostics.

members.name: warnings
members.doc_comment: Get all warning diagnostics.

members.name: by_kind
members.doc_comment: Get diagnostics of a specific `DiagnosticKind`.
members.type_signature: <K extends DiagnosticKind>(kind: K): (Extract<TypeExtractionDiagnostic, { kind: K; }> | Extract<SignatureAnalysisDiagnostic, { ...; }> | Extract<...> | Extract<...> | Extract<...>)[]
members.return_type: (Extract<TypeExtractionDiagnostic, { kind: K; }> | Extract<SignatureAnalysisDiagnostic, { kind: K; }> | Extract<...> | Extract<...> | Extract<...>)[]

declarations.name: FormatDiagnosticOptions
declarations.doc_comment: Options for formatting diagnostics.

properties.name: prefix
properties.doc_comment: Prefix for file path (default: './').

properties.name: strip_base
properties.doc_comment: Base path to strip from absolute file paths (e.g., process.cwd()).

declarations.name: format_diagnostic
declarations.doc_comment: Format a diagnostic for display.
declarations.return_description: formatted string like './file.ts:10:5: error: message'

declarations.name: autofocus
declarations.doc_comment:
Creates an attachment that focuses the element on mount.
Use this instead of the HTML `autofocus` attribute for elements
that mount dynamically from reactive conditionals like `{#if}`.

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: focus options forwarded to `element.focus()`; defaults to `{focusVisible: true}` to show focus indicators on programmatic focus

modules.module_comment: Svelte 5 attachment that focuses an element on mount.

props.name: path
props.description: Prefixed with a slash and relative to the base path.

props.name: selected_path
props.description:
Prefixed with a slash and relative to the base path.
`null` means none and `undefined` is detected from the current url.

props.name: base_path
props.description:
Sets a custom base path of `path` and `selected_path`.
Defaults to `base` from `$app/paths`.

declarations.name: create_context
declarations.doc_comment:
Wraps Svelte's `setContext` and `getContext` for better ergonomics.
When no value is set in the context,
`get` throws an error and `get_maybe` returns `undefined`.
If a `fallback` is provided, the `value` argument to `set` is optional
and `get_maybe` is omitted from the type.
declarations.type_signature: <T>(fallback: () => T): { get: () => T; set: (value?: T | undefined) => T; }
declarations.return_type: { get: () => T; set: (value?: T | undefined) => T; }

members.name: has_custom_layout

If an initial layout is provided, control is deferred externally.
Otherwise the layout syncs to the page dimensions.

members.name: open
members.type_signature: (params: ContextmenuParams[], x: number, y: number): void

parameters.name: params
parameters.description: contextmenu parameters or nullish to disable

declarations.name: contextmenu_open
declarations.doc_comment:
Opens the contextmenu, if appropriate,
querying the menu items from the DOM starting at the event target.
declarations.type_signature: (target: HTMLElement | SVGElement, x: number, y: number, contextmenu: ContextmenuState, options?: ContextmenuOpenOptions | undefined): boolean
declarations.return_description: a boolean indicating if the menu was opened or not

parameters.name: target
parameters.description: the leaf element from which to open the contextmenu

parameters.name: x
parameters.description: the page X coordinate at which to open the contextmenu, typically the mouse `pageX`

parameters.name: y
parameters.description: the page Y coordinate at which to open the contextmenu, typically the mouse `pageY`

parameters.name: options
parameters.description: optional configuration for filtering entries and haptic feedback

declarations.doc_comment:
Touch event handler for tap-then-longpress bypass detection.

This allows users to access the native context menu by performing a tap
followed by a longpress/rightclick within a specified time window.
The bypass gesture is useful for accessing browser features like text selection
or the native context menu when the Fuz contextmenu would normally override it.

Note: preventDefault is not called as we're only observing touch patterns,
not intercepting them. The actual bypass happens in on_window_contextmenu.

props.name: open_offset_x
props.description:
The number of pixels to offset from the pointer X position when opened.
Useful to ensure the first menu item is immediately under the pointer.

props.name: open_offset_y
props.description:
The number of pixels to offset from the pointer Y position when opened.
Useful to ensure the first menu item is immediately under the pointer.

props.name: bypass_with_tap_then_longpress
props.description:
Whether to detect tap-then-longpress to bypass the Fuz contextmenu.
This allows access to the system contextmenu by tapping once then rightclicking/long-pressing.
Setting to `false` disables the gesture.

props.name: bypass_window
props.description:
The number of milliseconds between taps to detect a gesture that bypasses the Fuz contextmenu.
Used only when `bypass_with_tap_then_longpress` is true.
If the duration is too long, it'll detect more false positives and interrupt normal usage,
but too short and some people will have difficulty performing the gesture.

props.name: bypass_move_tolerance
props.description:
The number of pixels the pointer can be moved between taps to detect a tap-then-longpress.
Used only when `bypass_with_tap_then_longpress` is true.

props.name: scoped
props.description: If `true`, wraps `children` with a div and listens to events on it instead of the window.

props.name: link_entry
props.description:
Snippet for rendering link entries.
Set to `null` to disable automatic link detection.
Defaults to `link_entry_default` which renders `ContextmenuLinkEntry`.

props.name: text_entry
props.description:
Snippet for rendering copy text entries.
Set to `null` to disable automatic copy text detection.
Defaults to `text_entry_default` which renders `ContextmenuTextEntry`.

props.name: separator_entry
props.description:
Snippet for rendering separator entries.
Set to `null` to disable automatic separator rendering.
Defaults to `separator_entry_default` which renders `ContextmenuSeparator`.

declarations.name: csp_directives_of_fuzdev
declarations.doc_comment:
Per-directive sources that allow full interop between fuzdev/sister sites
(`*.fuz.dev`, `*.zzz.software`) — loading assets and APIs from each other,
and iframing in both directions.

Pass into `create_csp_directives({extend: [csp_directives_of_fuzdev]})`.
Intended for sites within the ecosystem; external apps usually want a
narrower, hand-written allow-list.

Deliberately scoped — `script-src`, `style-src`, `worker-src`, etc. are not
granted, even between sister sites. Add at the call site if a specific page
needs them.

declarations.name: CspDirectiveSourcesMap
declarations.doc_comment:
Per-directive map of source arrays — accepted as `extend` layer entries.
Excludes directives like `'upgrade-insecure-requests'` (boolean) that can't be appended to.

declarations.name: CreateCspDirectivesOptions
declarations.doc_comment:
Options for `create_csp_directives`.

The pipeline runs in three stages:
1. `replace_defaults` sets the starting state (defaults to `csp_directive_value_defaults`).
2. `extend` appends sources per directive, layered left to right.
3. `overrides` replaces or removes per-directive values as a final pass.

properties.name: replace_defaults
properties.doc_comment:
Starting values per directive — *wholesale replaces* the library defaults.

- Omitted: uses `csp_directive_value_defaults` (the curated library defaults).
- Provided: exactly the directives you list, nothing else inherited.
  Anything not listed is **absent** from the starting state — including security defaults
  like `default-src: 'none'`. To tweak a single directive while keeping the rest, use
  `extend` (to append) or `overrides` (to replace per-key) instead.
- `{}`: starts blank with no directives.

`null` is not accepted (top-level or per-key) — omit the option to use library defaults,
pass `{}` to start blank, or use `overrides` to remove a specific directive.

Per-key `undefined` is treated as omitted (no-op).

properties.name: extend
properties.doc_comment:
Sources to append per directive, layered left to right.
Each entry is a partial map; values append to the result of `replace_defaults` and prior entries.
Values are deduplicated within and across layers.

Only array-typed directives can be extended (boolean directives like `upgrade-insecure-requests`
are excluded by the type). Throws if any entry attempts to extend a directive whose current
value is `['none']` — use `replace_defaults` or `overrides` to opt into default-deny directives.

Per-key `undefined` is treated as omitted (no-op) — supports conditional patterns like
`{'connect-src': is_prod ? [API_URL] : undefined}`. Per-key `null` throws — `extend` only
appends; use `overrides: { 'X': null }` to remove a directive.

properties.name: overrides
properties.doc_comment:
Final-pass per-directive overrides. Replaces the directive value or removes it entirely.
Pass `null` to remove a directive from the output.

Highest precedence — wins over `replace_defaults` and `extend`.

Per-key `undefined` is treated as omitted (no-op) — distinct from `null`, which removes.

declarations.name: create_csp_directives
declarations.doc_comment:
Builds a CSP directives map for use with SvelteKit's `kit.csp.directives` option.

Restrictive by default; opt into specific permissions via `extend` (append) or
`overrides` (replace). Designed to read as an audit log: every user-added source
is named at exactly one site in the source code. Library defaults are inherited
unless you opt out via `replace_defaults`.

Validation:
- Unknown directive keys throw.
- Extending a `['none']` directive throws (use `replace_defaults`/`overrides` to opt in).
- `null` for `replace_defaults` (top-level or per-key) throws — omit the option for library
  defaults, pass `{}` to start blank, or use `overrides` to remove a specific directive.
- `null` per-key in `extend` throws (use `overrides` for removal).
- `undefined` per-key is treated as omitted in all three stages.
- Non-object entries in `extend` (`null`, `undefined`, primitives) throw with a friendly error.
- Output is validated to ensure `'none'` never appears alongside other tokens,
  that no directive ends up with an empty array (use `['none']` to forbid all),
  and that every source array contains only strings.

Things like rendering to a string are out of scope and left to SvelteKit.

declarations.name: csp_directive_value_defaults
declarations.doc_comment:
The library CSP directive defaults — directives enabled out of the box.
Prioritizes safety but loosens around media and styles, relying on defense-in-depth.
WASM compile is allowed (`'wasm-unsafe-eval'` on `script-src` and `worker-src`); `eval` is not.

Directives not listed here (`report-to`, `require-trusted-types-for`, `trusted-types`,
`sandbox`) are intentionally absent by default — opt in via `replace_defaults` or `overrides`.

Customizable via `CreateCspDirectivesOptions.replace_defaults`.
declarations.type_signature: Partial<{ "default-src": (CspSource | CspActionSource)[]; "script-src": (CspSource | CspActionSource)[]; "script-src-elem": CspSources; ... 20 more ...; sandbox: ("allow-downloads-without-user-activation" | ... 11 more ... | "allow-top-navigation-by-user-activation")[]; }>

declarations.name: csp_directive_specs
declarations.doc_comment:
Static data descriptors for the CSP directives.
Fuz excludes deprecated directives, so those are intentionally omitted,
but any newer missing directives are bugs.

enabling transitions and improving performance, sometimes significantly.
Pass `true` to render children eagerly to gain
`ctrl+f` functionality while trading away the benefits.
Consider a `<details>` element instead of making this component eager.
