properties.name: repo_url
properties.doc_comment: The HTTPS URL to the repo. Does not include a `.git` suffix.

properties.name: repo_dir
properties.doc_comment:
Relative or absolute path to the repo's local directory.
If `null`, the directory is inferred from the URL and cwd.

properties.name: branch
properties.doc_comment: The branch name to use when fetching the repo. Defaults to `main`.

declarations.name: normalize_gitops_config
declarations.doc_comment:
Transforms a `RawGitopsConfig` to the more strict `GitopsConfig`.
This allows users to provide a more relaxed config.

declarations.name: validate_gitops_config_module
declarations.type_signature: (config_module: any, config_path: string): asserts config_module is GitopsConfigModule

modules.module_comment:
Configuration types and normalization for gitops multi-repo management.

Two-phase configuration system:
- `RawGitopsConfig` - User-friendly format with optional fields and flexible types
- `GitopsConfig` - Internal format with required fields and strict types

This allows users to provide minimal configs (e.g., just URLs as strings) while
the system works with normalized configs internally for type safety.

declarations.name: GITOPS_MAX_ITERATIONS_DEFAULT
declarations.doc_comment:
Maximum number of iterations for fixed-point iteration during publishing.
Used in both plan generation and actual publishing to resolve transitive dependency cascades.

In practice, most repos converge in 2-3 iterations.
Deep dependency chains may require more iterations.

declarations.name: GITOPS_CONFIG_PATH_DEFAULT
declarations.doc_comment: Default path to the gitops configuration file.

declarations.name: GITOPS_CONCURRENCY_DEFAULT
declarations.doc_comment: Default number of repos to process concurrently during parallel operations.

declarations.name: GITOPS_NPM_WAIT_TIMEOUT_DEFAULT
declarations.doc_comment:
Default timeout in milliseconds for waiting on NPM package propagation (10 minutes).
NPM's CDN uses eventual consistency, so published packages may not be immediately available.

modules.module_comment:
Shared constants for gitops tasks and operations.

Naming convention: GITOPS_{NAME}_DEFAULT for user-facing defaults.

declarations.name: Args
declarations.type_signature: ZodObject<{ command: ZodString; config: ZodDefault<ZodString>; concurrency: ZodDefault<ZodNumber>; format: ZodDefault<ZodEnum<{ json: "json"; text: "text"; }>>; }, $strict>

declarations.name: task
declarations.type_signature: Task<{ command: string; config: string; concurrency: number; format: "json" | "text"; }, ZodType<Args, Args, $ZodTypeInternals<Args, Args>>, unknown>

declarations.name: get_gitops_ready
declarations.doc_comment:
Central initialization function for all gitops tasks.

Initialization sequence:
1. Loads and normalizes config from `gitops.config.ts`
2. Resolves local repo paths (creates missing with `--download`)
3. Switches branches and pulls latest changes (in parallel by default)
4. Auto-installs deps if `package.json` changed during pull

Priority for path resolution:
- `dir` argument (explicit override)
- Config `repos_dir` setting
- `DEFAULT_REPOS_DIR` constant

throws.description: config loading or repo resolution fails

declarations.type_signature: (options: GetGitopsReadyOptions): Promise<{ config_path: string; repos_dir: string; gitops_config: GitopsConfig; local_repos: LocalRepo[]; }>
declarations.return_type: Promise<{ config_path: string; repos_dir: string; gitops_config: GitopsConfig; local_repos: LocalRepo[]; }>
declarations.return_description: initialized config and fully loaded repos ready for operations

modules.module_comment:
Shared initialization logic for all gitops tasks.

Provides `get_gitops_ready()` which orchestrates:
- Config loading and normalization
- Repo resolution (local path discovery)
- Branch switching and syncing
- Dependency installation

Used by: `gitops_sync.task.ts`, `gitops_analyze.task.ts`, `gitops_plan.task.ts`,
`gitops_publish.task.ts`, and `gitops_validate.task.ts`.

Accepts `git_ops` and `npm_ops` parameters to support testing via operations pattern
(see `operations.ts` for dependency injection details).

declarations.name: validate_dependency_graph
declarations.doc_comment:
Shared utility for building dependency graph, detecting cycles, and computing publishing order.
This centralizes logic that was duplicated across `multi_repo_publisher`, `publishing_plan`, and `gitops_analyze`.

throws.description: production cycles detected and `throw_on_prod_cycles` is true

declarations.type_signature: (repos: LocalRepo[], options?: { log?: Logger | undefined; throw_on_prod_cycles?: boolean | undefined; log_cycles?: boolean | undefined; log_order?: boolean | undefined; }): GraphValidationResult
declarations.return_description: graph validation result with graph, publishing order, and detected cycles

parameters.name: options
parameters.type: { log?: Logger | undefined; throw_on_prod_cycles?: boolean | undefined; log_cycles?: boolean | undefined; log_order?: boolean | undefined; }

modules.module_comment:
Shared dependency graph validation logic used across multiple workflows.

Consolidates graph building, cycle detection, and publishing order computation
that was duplicated in three places: `multi_repo_publisher.ts`, `publishing_plan.ts`,
and `gitops_analyze.task.ts`.

Options pattern supports different behaviors: analyze can tolerate cycles for
reporting, while publish must throw on production cycles.

See also: `dependency_graph.ts` for core graph data structure and algorithms.

declarations.name: LocalRepo
declarations.doc_comment:
Fully loaded local repo with `Library` and extracted dependency data.
Does not extend `LocalRepoPath` - `Library` is source of truth for name/repo_url/etc.

declarations.name: LocalRepoPath
declarations.doc_comment:
A repo that has been located on the filesystem (path exists).
Used before loading - just filesystem/git concerns.

declarations.name: LocalRepoMissing
declarations.doc_comment: A repo that is missing from the filesystem (needs cloning).

declarations.name: local_repo_load
declarations.doc_comment:
Loads repo data with automatic syncing and dependency management.

Workflow:
1. Records current commit hash (for detecting changes)
2. Switches to target branch if needed (requires clean workspace)
3. Pulls latest changes from remote (skipped for local-only repos)
4. Validates workspace is clean after pull
5. Auto-installs dependencies if `package.json` changed
6. Imports `library_json` from `src/routes/library.ts`
7. Creates `Library` and extracts dependency maps

This ensures repos are always in sync with their configured branch
before being used by gitops commands.

throws.description: workspace dirty, branch switch fails, install fails, or `library.ts` missing

declarations.type_signature: ({ local_repo_path, log: _log, git_ops, npm_ops, }: { local_repo_path: LocalRepoPath; log?: Logger | undefined; git_ops?: GitOperations | undefined; npm_ops?: NpmOperations | undefined; }): Promise<...>

parameters.type: { local_repo_path: LocalRepoPath; log?: Logger | undefined; git_ops?: GitOperations | undefined; npm_ops?: NpmOperations | undefined; }

parameters.type: { resolved_config: ResolvedGitopsConfig; repos_dir: string; gitops_config: GitopsConfig; download: boolean; log?: Logger | undefined; npm_ops?: NpmOperations | undefined; }

declarations.name: local_repos_load
declarations.type_signature: ({ local_repo_paths, log, git_ops, npm_ops, parallel, concurrency, }: { local_repo_paths: LocalRepoPath[]; log?: Logger | undefined; git_ops?: GitOperations | undefined; npm_ops?: NpmOperations | undefined; parallel?: boolean | undefined; concurrency?: number | undefined; }): Promise<...>

parameters.type: { local_repo_paths: LocalRepoPath[]; log?: Logger | undefined; git_ops?: GitOperations | undefined; npm_ops?: NpmOperations | undefined; parallel?: boolean | undefined; concurrency?: number | undefined; }

Logs dev circular dependencies as info.

Logs a simple bulleted list with a header.
Common pattern for warnings, info messages, and other lists.
declarations.type_signature: (items: string[], header: string, color: "cyan" | "yellow" | "red" | "dim", log: Logger, log_method?: "error" | "info" | "warn"): void

declarations.name: install_with_cache_healing
declarations.doc_comment:
Installs npm dependencies with cache healing on ETARGET errors.

**Strategy:**
1. First attempt: regular `npm install`
2. On ETARGET error (stale cache): `npm cache clean --force` then retry
3. On other errors: fail immediately

**Why ETARGET errors occur:**
After publishing a package and waiting for NPM registry propagation,
npm's local cache may still have stale "404" metadata. This healing
strategy clears the cache to force fresh metadata fetch.

throws.description: if install fails (with details about cache healing attempts)

declarations.type_signature: (repo: LocalRepo, ops: GitopsOperations, log?: Logger | undefined): Promise<void>

parameters.name: repo
parameters.description: the repository to install dependencies for

declarations.name: check_package_available
declarations.type_signature: (pkg: string, version: string, options?: { log?: Logger | undefined; }): Promise<boolean>

declarations.name: wait_for_package
declarations.doc_comment:
Waits for package version to propagate to NPM registry.

Uses exponential backoff with jitter to avoid hammering registry.
Logs progress every 5 attempts. Respects timeout to avoid infinite waits.

Critical for multi-repo publishing: ensures published packages are available
before updating dependent packages.

throws.description: timeout reached or max attempts exceeded

declarations.type_signature: (pkg: string, version: string, options?: WaitOptions): Promise<void>

declarations.name: get_package_info
declarations.doc_comment:
Fetches package metadata from NPM registry.

Returns name and latest version. Returns null if package doesn't exist
or registry is unreachable.
declarations.type_signature: (pkg: string, options?: { log?: Logger | undefined; }): Promise<PackageInfo | null>
declarations.return_description: package info or null on error/not found

declarations.name: package_exists
declarations.type_signature: (pkg: string, options?: { log?: Logger | undefined; }): Promise<boolean>

declarations.name: default_gitops_operations
declarations.doc_comment: Combined default operations for all gitops functionality.

modules.module_comment:
Production implementations of operations interfaces.

Provides real git, npm, fs, and build operations for production use.
For interface definitions and dependency injection pattern, see `operations.ts`.

declarations.name: ChangesetOperations
declarations.doc_comment: Changeset operations for reading and predicting versions from `.changeset/*.md` files.

properties.name: has_changesets
properties.doc_comment:
Checks if a repo has any changeset files.
Returns true if changesets exist, false if none found.

properties.name: read_changesets
properties.doc_comment:
Reads all changeset files from a repo.
Returns array of changeset info, or error if reading fails.

properties.name: predict_next_version
properties.doc_comment:
Predicts the next version based on changesets.
Returns null if no changesets found (expected, not an error).
Returns error `Result` if changesets exist but can't be read/parsed.

declarations.name: GitOperations
declarations.doc_comment:
Git operations for branch management, commits, tags, and workspace state.
All operations return `Result` instead of throwing errors.

properties.name: current_branch_name
properties.doc_comment: Gets the current branch name.

properties.name: current_commit_hash
properties.doc_comment: Gets the current commit hash.

properties.name: check_clean_workspace
properties.doc_comment: Checks if the workspace is clean (no uncommitted changes).

properties.name: checkout
properties.doc_comment: Checks out a branch.

properties.name: pull
properties.doc_comment: Pulls changes from remote.

properties.name: switch_branch
properties.doc_comment: Switches to a branch, optionally pulling.

properties.name: has_remote
properties.doc_comment: Checks if a remote exists.

properties.name: add
properties.doc_comment: Stages files for commit.

properties.name: commit
properties.doc_comment: Creates a commit.

properties.name: add_and_commit
properties.doc_comment: Stages files and creates a commit.

properties.name: has_changes
properties.doc_comment: Checks if there are any uncommitted changes.

properties.name: list_uncommitted_files
properties.doc_comment:
Lists uncommitted files in the working tree (`git diff --name-only HEAD`).

Renamed from `get_changed_files` in 2026-04 because "changed files" collided
with mageguild's `get_changed_files` which diffs two refs. This one reports
uncommitted working-tree changes relative to HEAD.

properties.name: tag
properties.doc_comment: Creates a git tag.

properties.name: push_tag
properties.doc_comment: Pushes a tag to remote.

properties.name: stash
properties.doc_comment: Stashes uncommitted changes.

properties.name: stash_pop
properties.doc_comment: Pops the most recent stash.

properties.name: has_file_changed
properties.doc_comment: Checks if a specific file changed between two commits.

declarations.name: ProcessOperations
declarations.doc_comment: Process spawning operations for running shell commands.

properties.name: spawn
properties.doc_comment: Spawns a child process and waits for completion.

declarations.name: BuildOperations
declarations.doc_comment: Build operations for validating packages compile before publishing.

properties.name: build_package
properties.doc_comment: Builds a package using `gro build`.

declarations.name: NpmOperations
declarations.doc_comment:
NPM registry operations for package availability checks and authentication.
Includes exponential backoff for waiting on package propagation.

properties.name: wait_for_package
properties.doc_comment:
Waits for a package version to be available on NPM.
Uses exponential backoff with configurable timeout.

properties.name: check_package_available
properties.doc_comment: Checks if a package version is available on NPM.

properties.name: check_auth
properties.doc_comment: Checks npm authentication status.

properties.name: check_registry
properties.doc_comment: Checks if npm registry is reachable.

properties.name: install
properties.doc_comment: Installs npm dependencies.

properties.name: cache_clean
properties.doc_comment:
Cleans the npm cache.
Uses `npm cache clean --force` to clear stale cache entries.

declarations.name: PreflightOperations
declarations.doc_comment:
Preflight validation operations to ensure repos are ready for publishing.
Validates workspace state, branches, builds, and npm authentication.

properties.name: run_preflight_checks
properties.doc_comment: Runs preflight validation checks before publishing.

declarations.name: FsOperations
declarations.doc_comment:
File system operations for reading and writing files.

Errors are typed via `FsError` (`not_found | permission_denied |
already_exists | io_error`) so callers can branch on `kind` instead of
regex-matching `message`. See `@fuzdev/fuz_util/fs.js`.

properties.name: readFile
properties.doc_comment: Reads a file from the file system.

properties.name: writeFile
properties.doc_comment: Writes a file to the file system.

properties.name: mkdir
properties.doc_comment: Creates a directory, optionally with recursive creation.

properties.name: exists
properties.doc_comment: Checks if a path exists on the file system.

declarations.name: GitopsOperations
declarations.doc_comment:
Combined operations interface grouping all gitops functionality.
This is the main interface injected into publishing and validation workflows.

properties.name: stdout
properties.doc_comment: This function should call log methods directly for colored/styled output.

declarations.name: format_and_output
declarations.doc_comment:
Formats data and outputs to file or stdout based on options.

Supports three formats:
- stdout: Uses logger for colored/styled output (cannot use with `--outfile`)
- json: Stringified JSON
- markdown: Formatted markdown text

throws.description: stdout format used with `outfile`, or if logger missing for stdout

declarations.type_signature: <T>(data: T, formatters: OutputFormatters<T>, options: OutputOptions): Promise<void>

declarations.name: GITOPS_OUTPUT_DIR
declarations.doc_comment: Base directory for all gitops-generated files.

declarations.name: DEFAULT_REPOS_DIR
declarations.doc_comment:
Default repos directory relative to gitops config file.
Resolves to the parent of the directory with the config
(e.g., `~/dev/repo/gitops.config.ts` resolves to `~/dev/`).

declarations.name: run_preflight_checks
declarations.doc_comment:
Validates all requirements before publishing can proceed.

Performs comprehensive pre-flight validation:
- Clean workspaces (100% clean required - no uncommitted changes)
- Correct branch (usually main)
- Changesets present (unless `skip_changesets`=true)
- Builds successful (fail-fast to prevent broken state)
- Git remote reachability
- NPM authentication with username
- NPM registry connectivity

Build validation runs BEFORE any publishing to prevent the scenario where
version is bumped but build fails, leaving repo in broken state.
declarations.type_signature: ({ repos, preflight_options, git_ops, npm_ops, build_ops, changeset_ops, }: RunPreflightChecksOptions): Promise<PreflightResult>
declarations.return_description: result with `ok`=false if any errors, plus warnings and detailed status

declarations.name: calculate_dependency_updates
declarations.doc_comment:
Calculates all dependency updates between packages based on predicted versions.

Iterates through all repos, checking prod, peer, and dev dependencies to find
which packages will need dependency version bumps after publishing.

Also tracks "breaking cascades" - when a breaking change propagates to dependents.
declarations.type_signature: (repos: LocalRepo[], predicted_versions: Map<string, string>, breaking_packages: Set<string>): { dependency_updates: DependencyUpdate[]; breaking_cascades: Map<...>; }

declarations.name: get_required_bump_for_dependencies
declarations.doc_comment:
Determines the required bump type for a package based on its dependency updates.

Returns null if no prod/peer dependency updates, otherwise returns the minimum
required bump type (major for breaking deps, patch otherwise).

Respects pre-1.0 semver conventions (minor for breaking in 0.x).
declarations.type_signature: (repo: LocalRepo, dependency_updates: DependencyUpdate[], breaking_packages: Set<string>): BumpType | null

Walk files in a directory, respecting common exclusions.

Get repo paths from gitops config without full git sync.

modules.module_comment:
Generic repository operations for scripts that work across repos.

Provides lightweight utilities for:
- Getting repo paths from gitops config (without full git sync)
- Walking files in repos with sensible exclusions
- Common exclusion patterns for node/svelte projects

For full git sync/clone functionality, use `get_gitops_ready()` from `gitops_task_helpers`.

declarations.name: RepoJson
declarations.doc_comment: Serialized repo data as stored in `repos.ts` (JSON).

declarations.name: Repo

Runtime repo with `Library` composition for package metadata.

Wraps a `Library` instance and adds GitHub-specific data (CI status, PRs).
Convenience getters delegate to `this.library.*` for common properties.

declarations.name: BumpType
declarations.doc_comment: Semantic Versioning 2.0.0 utilities

declarations.name: semver_compare_versions
declarations.doc_comment:
Compares two semver versions according to SemVer 2.0.0 spec.
Returns -1 if a < b, 0 if a === b, 1 if a > b.
Build metadata is ignored in precedence comparison.

declarations.name: semver_bump_version
declarations.doc_comment:
Bumps a version according to the specified type.
Resets lower version numbers per SemVer spec.

modules.module_comment:
Semantic Versioning 2.0.0 utilities
@see https://semver.org/

properties.name: dependencies
version_changes
dependency_updates

declarations.name: serialize_graph
declarations.doc_comment: Serializes a dependency graph to a JSON-safe format.

modules.module_comment:
JSON-serializable types for command output formats.

Gitops commands support `--format json` and `--format markdown` output modes
in addition to styled terminal output. These types define the JSON schema for:
- Dependency graph structures (`SerializedGraph`)
- Publishing plan predictions (`SerializedPublishingPlan`)

Used by `gitops_analyze`, `gitops_plan`, and `gitops_publish` (dry run) when
`--format json` or `--outfile` is specified.

declarations.name: strip_version_prefix
declarations.doc_comment: Strips version prefix (^, ~, >=, <=, etc) from a version string.

declarations.name: get_version_prefix
declarations.doc_comment: Gets the version prefix (^, ~, >=, <=, or empty string).

declarations.name: normalize_version_for_comparison
declarations.doc_comment:
Normalizes version string for comparison.

Strips prefixes (^, ~, >=) to get bare version number.
Handles wildcards as-is. Used by `needs_update` to compare versions.
examples:
```ts
normalize_version_for_comparison('^1.2.3') // '1.2.3'
```

```ts
normalize_version_for_comparison('>=2.0.0') // '2.0.0'
```

```ts
normalize_version_for_comparison('*') // '*'
```

declarations.name: get_update_prefix
declarations.doc_comment:
Determines version prefix to use when updating dependencies.

Strategy:
- Wildcard (*): Use caret (^) as default
- Has existing prefix: Preserve it (^, ~, >=, <=, etc)
- No prefix: Use default_strategy

This preserves user intent while handling wildcard replacements sensibly.

parameters.name: default_strategy
parameters.description: prefix to use when no existing prefix found

declarations.name: is_breaking_change
declarations.doc_comment:
Determines if a bump is a breaking change based on semver rules.
Pre-1.0: minor bumps are breaking
1.0+: major bumps are breaking

declarations.name: compare_bump_types
declarations.doc_comment: Compares bump types. Returns positive if a > b, negative if a < b, 0 if equal.

declarations.name: FrontendActionsApi
declarations.doc_comment:
Typed dispatch surface for the frontend's RPC client. Symmetric counterpart
of `BackendActionsApi`. Async methods (request_response, remote_notification,
async local_call) return `Promise<Result<...>>` and accept an optional
`RpcClientCallOptions` second arg that threads `signal`, `transport_name`,
and `queue` through to the peer. Sync local_call methods return values
directly.

declarations.name: ProgressMeta
declarations.doc_comment:
Progress-token envelope shared by streaming actions. Carries the caller's
`progressToken` so streamed `*_progress` notifications can be correlated
with the originating request. `looseObject` lets transports tack on
additional MCP-style metadata without a schema bump.

declarations.name: PingOutput
declarations.doc_comment: Output for `ping`. Echoes the JSON-RPC request id back as `ping_id`.

declarations.name: SessionLoadData
declarations.doc_comment:
Inner payload of `session_load`. The fields here are the actual session
snapshot — files, scoped dirs, provider status, workspaces.

TODO extract to `diskfile_types.ts` (or a session-specific module) once
the Rust backend grows its own typed session response and we can move
this schema to the shared boundary.
declarations.type_signature: ZodObject<{ zzz_dir: $ZodBranded<ZodPipe<ZodPipe<ZodString, ZodTransform<string, string>>, $ZodBranded<ZodString, "DiskfilePath", "out">>, "DiskfileDirectoryPath", "out">; scoped_dirs: ZodReadonly<...>; files: ZodArray<...>; provider_status: ZodArray<...>; workspaces: ZodArray<...>; }, $strict>

declarations.name: SessionLoadOutput
declarations.doc_comment:
Output for `session_load`. The `data` wrapper is historical and kept for
cross-backend parity: the Rust backend serializes this exact shape
(`crates/zzz_server/src/handlers.rs::SessionLoadResult`) and three
integration tests in `test/integration/tests.ts` read `result.data.*`
directly. Flattening would break parity in both places, so the wrapper
stays until the Rust side moves alongside.

declarations.name: all_action_specs
declarations.doc_comment:
Canonical action spec list — fuz_app's protocol actions (`heartbeat`,
`cancel`) plus zzz-owned specs. Single source of truth for codegen and
runtime registries (`Frontend.action_registry`, `Backend.action_registry`).

Codegen helpers default-filter protocol actions out of typed surfaces
(`FrontendActionsApi`, `BackendActionHandlers`, `ActionMethod`, etc.) since
they're dispatcher-owned. Server WS registration pairs protocol specs with
fuz_app-shipped handlers via `protocol_actions`; iteration over
`all_action_specs` skips them via `is_protocol_action_method` to avoid
double-registration. Adding a future protocol action requires no consumer
edit — fuz_app's `protocol_action_specs` is the upstream canonical bundle.
declarations.type_signature: ({ method: string; initiator: "both" | "frontend" | "backend"; side_effects: boolean; input: ZodType<unknown, unknown, $ZodTypeInternals<unknown, unknown>>; output: ZodType<unknown, unknown, $ZodTypeInternals<unknown, unknown>>; ... 4 more ...; streams?: string | undefined; } | { ...; } | { ...; })[]

declarations.name: Action
declarations.doc_comment: Represents a single action in the system, tracking its full lifecycle through action events.

members.name: pending
members.doc_comment:
Returns true if the action is still pending (not in a terminal state).
An action is complete when it reaches a terminal phase (where next phase is null)
AND the step is terminal (handled or failed).

members.name: success
members.doc_comment:
Returns true if the action completed successfully.
Success means: action is complete (terminal phase + step), step is 'handled', and no error.

declarations.name: Capability
declarations.doc_comment: Generic interface for a capability with standardized status tracking.

properties.name: name
properties.doc_comment: The capability name.

properties.name: data
properties.doc_comment: The capability's status: undefined=not initialized, null=checking, otherwise available or not.

properties.name: status
properties.doc_comment: Async status tracking the connection/check state.

properties.name: message_id
properties.doc_comment: Message id of the last request for this capability's info, if any.

properties.name: error_message
properties.doc_comment: Error message if any.

properties.name: updated
properties.doc_comment: Timestamp when the capability was last checked.

declarations.name: Capabilities
declarations.doc_comment:
A class that encapsulates system capabilities detection and management.
This is NOT generic or extensible - it contains hardcoded logic for
all capabilities the system supports.

members.name: websocket
members.doc_comment: WebSocket capability that derives its state from the socket.

members.name: filesystem
members.doc_comment: The filesystem capability derives its state from the backend and `zzz_dir`.

members.name: ollama
members.doc_comment:
Ollama capability that derives its state from provider_status (authoritative)
and app.ollama (for richer data when available).

members.name: claude
members.doc_comment: Claude capability that derives its state from provider_status.

members.name: chatgpt
members.doc_comment: ChatGPT capability that derives its state from provider_status.

members.name: gemini
members.doc_comment: Gemini capability that derives its state from provider_status.

members.name: pings
members.doc_comment: Store pings - both pending and completed.

members.name: latest_ping_time
members.doc_comment: Most recent completed ping round trip time in milliseconds.

members.name: completed_pings
members.doc_comment: Completed pings (for display).

members.name: pending_ping_count
members.doc_comment: Number of pending pings.

members.name: has_pending_pings
members.doc_comment: Has pending pings.

members.name: backend_available
members.doc_comment:
Convenience accessor for backend availability.
`undefined` means uninitialized, `null` means loading/checking.
boolean indicates if available.

members.name: ollama_available
members.doc_comment:
Convenience accessor for ollama availability.
`undefined` means uninitialized, `null` means loading/checking.
boolean indicates if available.

members.name: websocket_available
members.doc_comment:
Convenience accessor for websocket availability.
`undefined` means uninitialized, `null` means loading/checking.
boolean indicates if the socket is actively connected.

members.name: filesystem_available
members.doc_comment:
Convenience accessor for filesystem availability.
`undefined` means uninitialized, `null` means loading/checking.
boolean indicates if filesystem is available.

members.name: ollama_models
members.doc_comment: Latest Ollama model list response, if available.

declarations.name: CellRegistry
declarations.doc_comment:
Registry for managing cell classes and their instances.
The goal is to allow dynamic instantiation of all cells from serializable JSON.
`CellRegistry` does not currently justify its weight/complexity and may be removed in the future,
but I want to continue exploring the ideas behind it until we get fully snapshottable UI.

members.doc_comment: Register a cell class with the registry.

members.name: unregister
members.doc_comment: Unregister a cell class from the registry.

members.name: maybe_instantiate
members.doc_comment:

declarations.name: get_global_cell_count
declarations.doc_comment:
A monotonic id for cell instances on the client.
This is not reactive and never changes on the instance,
whereas `id` can change. It's useful for ordering -
the motivating usecase was correctly sorting objects with the same `created` millisecond.

This may cause issues based on how data gets queried, but at least it's stable once loaded.
However we may need to revisit this if it causes problems.

declarations.name: Cell
declarations.doc_comment:
The `Cell` is the base class for schema-driven data models in Zzz.
The goals are still evolving, but a main idea is to have fully reactive state
that can be flexibly snapshotted and reinstantiated in a typesafe, extensible system
that uses normal Svelte patterns.

The design aims for:

- Integration with Svelte's reactivity, encouraging single-depth inheritance
		with Svelte class patterns for both persistent and ephemeral state
- Schema-driven parsing/validation and JSON serialization/deserialization
		(supporting snapshot and restore/replay patterns) via Zod
- Custom property encoding/decoding for complex types,
		and no boilerplate for schema-inferrable properties
- Lifecycle management with generic instantiation/registration and disposal
		(conceptually a WIP, partially implemented)
- Runtime type metadata for reflection

Cells are automatically registered in the global registry by `id`,
making them discoverable and referenceable throughout the system.
Each cell has common properties including `id` and `created`/`updated` timestamps.

There are currently a lot of rough edges and missing features!
My hope is that this could be generic enough to extract to a library, but it's not there yet.
I assume there's a really nice design in this space that takes full advantage of Svelte runes.

Many things will be possible with this pattern, but it's still a work in progress.

members.name: decoders
members.doc_comment:
Type-safe decoders for custom field decoding.
Override in subclasses to handle special field types.

Each decoder function takes a value of any type and should either:
1. Return a value (including null) to be assigned to the property
2. Return undefined to use the default decoding behavior
3. Return `HANDLED` to indicate the decoder has fully processed the property
   (virtual properties MUST return `HANDLED` if they exist in schema but not in class)

members.name: constructor
members.type_signature: <TSchema extends z.ZodType = ZodType<unknown, unknown, $ZodTypeInternals<unknown, unknown>>>(schema: TSchema, options: CellOptions<TSchema>): Cell<TSchema>

members.name: init
members.doc_comment:
Initialize the instance with `options.json` data if provided.
Must be called before using the instance -
the current pattern is calling it at the end of subclass constructors.

We should investigate deferring to callers so e.g. instantiating from the registry
would init automatically, subclasses need no init logic (which can get unwieldy with inheritance),
and then callers could always do custom init patterns if they wanted.

Can't be called automatically by the `Cell`'s constructor because
the subclass' constructor needs to run first to support field initialization.
A design goal behind the `Cell` is to support normal TS and Svelte patterns
with the most power and least intrusion. (there's a balance to find from Zzz's POV)

members.name: dispose
members.doc_comment: Clean up resources when this cell is no longer needed.

members.name: [Symbol.dispose]
members.doc_comment:
This is not supported in Safari, don't rely on this yet.
Uncomment temporarily to experiment in dev.

members.name: register
members.doc_comment:
Register this cell in the global cell registry.
Called automatically by `init()`.

members.name: unregister
members.doc_comment:
Unregister this cell from the global cell registry.
Called automatically by `dispose()`.

members.name: toJSON
members.doc_comment: For Svelte's $snapshot.

members.name: to_json
members.doc_comment:
Encodes the cell's serializable state as JSON.
Use the derived `cell.json` if you don't need a fresh copy.

members.name: set_json
members.doc_comment:
Apply JSON data to this instance.
Overwrites all properties including 'id'.
Special-cases `created` and `updated` for synchronization.

members.name: set_json_partial
members.doc_comment:
Update only the specified properties on this instance.
Preserves current values for any properties not included in the input.

members.name: encode_property
members.doc_comment:
Encode a value during serialization. Can be overridden for custom encoding logic.
Defaults to Svelte's `$state.snapshot`,
which handles most cases and uses `toJSON` when available,
so overriding `to_json` is sufficient for most cases before overriding `encode_property`.

members.name: decode_property
members.doc_comment:
Decode a value based on its schema type information.
This handles instantiating classes, transforming arrays, and special types.

Complex property types might require custom handling in parser functions
rather than using this general decoding mechanism.
members.type_signature: <K extends SchemaKeys<TSchema>>(value: unknown, key: K): any

members.name: assign_property
members.doc_comment:
Process a single schema property during JSON deserialization.
This method handles the workflow for mapping schema properties to instance properties.

Flow:
1. If a decoder exists for this property, try to use it
2. If decoder returns `HANDLED`, consider the property fully handled (short circuit)
3. If decoder returns a value other than `HANDLED` or undefined, use that value
4. If decoder returns undefined, fall through to standard decoding
5. For properties not directly represented in the class instance but defined
   in the schema, the decoder MUST return `HANDLED` to indicate proper handling

members.name: clone
members.doc_comment: Generic clone method that works for any subclass.
members.type_signature: (json?: input<TSchema> | undefined, options?: CellOptions<TSchema> | undefined): this

declarations.name: Ollama
declarations.doc_comment:
Ollama client state management with simplified API.
Model data is stored in `app.models`, not here.

## Status Display Pattern

For unified status display (available/unavailable/checking), components should use:
- `app.capabilities.ollama.status` - unified status (prioritizes provider status)
- `app.capabilities.ollama.error_message` - unified error message

This ensures consistency with other providers (Claude, ChatGPT, Gemini) and maintains
a single source of truth. The `Capabilities` layer prioritizes backend provider status
(authoritative - checks if Ollama is installed) over action-level status (checks if API responds).

The lower-level properties here (`list_status`, `list_error`, `provider_error`, etc.) are
operational state that `capabilities.ollama` derives from. Use these for:
- Operational state (models, pulling, creating, etc.)
- Action-specific error handling
- Internal state management

members.name: refresh
members.doc_comment:
Refresh the list of models and ps info and update the refresh timestamp.
Error handlers update state automatically.

If Ollama provider status indicates unavailability, skip API calls to avoid
overwriting the provider status with action error messages.

declarations.name: ENV_FILE
declarations.doc_comment:
Canonical env file path for daemon-initiated reads and writes.

The write target must match a file that's reloaded on next daemon start,
so user-entered values (e.g. API keys via `provider_update_api_key`)
survive restart:

- **Dev** (`gro dev`, `deno task dev`): both Vite and `scripts/dev.ts`
  reload `.env.development`. `.env` is shadowed by `.env.development` in
  Vite's mode-precedence and is not loaded at all by `scripts/dev.ts`,
  so writing to `.env` in dev would be invisible on restart.
- **Prod** (compiled `zzz daemon start`): `server/server.ts` loads `.env` via
  `load_env_file`. `.env.production` is NOT loaded by the compiled daemon
  (only by `deno task preview`'s `--env=.env.production` flag), so writing
  there would be invisible on restart.

modules.module_comment:

Mirror of `src/lib/constants.ts` for modules in the Deno compile chain.
Must not import `$env/static/public` or any SvelteKit build-time module —
those don't exist in Deno and crash `gro build` / `deno compile`.

declarations.type_signature: ({ path, app, backend, upgradeWebSocket, artificial_delay, transport, }: RegisterWebsocketActionsOptions): void

modules.module_comment:
WebSocket endpoint wiring — thin wrapper over fuz_app's
`register_action_ws`.

zzz supplies the action tuples (spec + handler) and a context-extender
that adds the domain `Backend` onto the base per-request context. All
dispatch, auth, validation, and transport bookkeeping live in fuz_app.

The shared `heartbeat_action` is spread first so disconnect
detection is identical across every fuz_app consumer.
zzz session configuration.

declarations.name: ScopedFsPath
declarations.doc_comment: A branded type for representing safely normalized filesystem paths

declarations.name: ScopedFs
declarations.doc_comment:
Provides a secure wrapper around filesystem operations to prevent path traversal attacks and
unauthorized file access.

Security features:
- Restricts operations to specified allowed paths
- Prevents path traversal attacks by normalizing all paths
- Blocks access to symlinks to avoid arbitrary file access
- Requires absolute paths to avoid relative path confusion
- Validates the entire path hierarchy for each operation

`ScopedFs` should be used whenever performing filesystem operations on
user-provided or untrusted input paths to ensure proper access boundaries.

declarations.doc_comment:
Addresses that bind to all network interfaces.

declarations.doc_comment: Check whether a bind address is a wildcard that exposes to the network.
declarations.return_description: true if the host binds to all interfaces

declarations.name: extract_hostname
declarations.doc_comment:
Extract the hostname portion from a Host header value.
Handles IPv6 brackets: `[::1]:3000` → `[::1]`
Handles regular: `localhost:3000` → `localhost`
declarations.return_description: the hostname without port

declarations.name: build_allowed_hostnames
declarations.doc_comment:
Build the set of allowed hostnames for Host header validation
based on the server's bind address.

When binding to `localhost` or `127.0.0.1`, both are allowed
(they refer to the same interface). When binding to `0.0.0.0`,
all local hostnames are allowed since we can't know which
interface the request arrived on.
declarations.return_description: set of hostnames to accept in the Host header

parameters.name: bind_host
parameters.description: the address the server is binding to

declarations.name: create_host_validation_middleware
declarations.doc_comment:
Create middleware that validates the Host header against an allowlist.

Blocks requests whose Host header hostname doesn't match any allowed value.
The port portion of the Host header is stripped before comparison.
Requests without a Host header are allowed through (non-browser clients like curl or CLI).
declarations.return_description: Hono middleware handler

modules.module_comment:
Host header validation middleware for DNS rebinding defense-in-depth.

Validates that the Host header matches expected local hostnames.
Requests without a Host header are allowed (HTTP/1.0, CLI tools).

Single entry point for both dev mode (`gro dev` via `gro_plugin_deno_server`)
and production (`zzz daemon start`). Uses the shared `create_zzz_app` factory
for the Hono app with fuz_app auth stack, then binds with `Deno.serve`
and handles daemon lifecycle.

declarations.name: Socket
declarations.doc_comment:
Wraps `FrontendWebsocketClient` with zzz-specific concerns: a
retryable fire-and-forget send queue (distinct from fuz_app's
request-level durable queue), URL input tracking, and a mapping from
fuz_app's `SocketStatus` onto zzz's `AsyncStatus`. Plain reactive class
— not a Cell. Implements `WebsocketRpcConnection` so it can back
`FrontendWebsocketTransport`; the `request` method is a one-line
delegate to the underlying `FrontendWebsocketClient`, keeping the
pending-request map in one canonical place.

The bespoke heartbeat timer has been retired — fuz_app's
`FrontendWebsocketClient` now ships an activity-aware heartbeat that
sends the shared `heartbeat_action` at `heartbeat_interval` idle.
Assigning `heartbeat_interval` pushes the new policy into the live
client immediately (the timer is restarted in place when connected).

Reconnect settings (`reconnect_delay`, `reconnect_delay_max`,
`auto_reconnect`) propagate to the underlying client on assignment
via `apply_reconnect_policy()` — in-flight waits are monotonically
shortened (never extended).

members.name: last_send_time
members.doc_comment:
UI timestamps for the "last send" / "last receive" diagnostics in
`CapabilityWebsocket`. Not used for heartbeat scheduling —
fuz_app's client owns that.

members.name: reconnect_attempt
members.doc_comment:
Changes each time a close fires — used by the UI as an animation key so
each reconnect wait restarts the progress bar.

members.name: request
members.doc_comment:
Delegate to the underlying `FrontendWebsocketClient.request` — keeps the
pending-request map, durable queue, and `AbortSignal` cancel in one
canonical place. Rejects when there is no client (call `connect()` first).
members.type_signature: (method: string, params?: unknown, options?: { signal?: AbortSignal | undefined; queue?: boolean | undefined; id?: string | number | undefined; } | undefined): Promise<unknown>

parameters.name: options
parameters.type: { signal?: AbortSignal | undefined; queue?: boolean | undefined; id?: string | number | undefined; } | undefined

members.name: apply_reconnect_policy
members.doc_comment:
Push the current `auto_reconnect` / `reconnect_delay` /
`reconnect_delay_max` fields to the underlying client. No-op when
there is no client (next `connect()` picks them up from construction).

declarations.name: Time
declarations.doc_comment:
Reactive time management class that provides time-related utilities.
Has a configurable update interval that defaults to
a full minute to minimize wasteful reactivity,
so it's suitable for any cases that need at best 1-minute precision, unless reconfigured.

members.name: DEFAULT_INTERVAL
members.doc_comment:
Default update interval in milliseconds (1 minute).
The idea is to minimize reactivity and CPU usage for a common use case.

members.name: now
members.doc_comment:
Current time that updates on the configured interval.
This is reactive and can be used in derived computations.

members.name: interval
members.doc_comment: The interval in milliseconds between time updates.

members.name: running
members.doc_comment: Whether the interval timer is currently running.

members.name: start
members.doc_comment: Starts the interval timer if it's not already running.

members.name: stop
members.doc_comment: Stops the interval timer if it's running.

members.name: restart
members.doc_comment: Restarts the interval timer with a new interval.

members.name: update_now
members.doc_comment: Updates the now to the current time immediately.

members.name: destroy
members.doc_comment: Override `Cell`'s destroy method to ensure timer cleanup.

props.description: Current state of the toggle

props.name: active_content
props.description: Content to display when toggle is active

props.name: inactive_content
props.description: Content to display when toggle is inactive

declarations.name: ZzzRuntime
declarations.doc_comment:
Unified runtime abstraction for zzz CLI operations.

Provides all runtime primitives as injectable dependencies.
Functions should accept partial interfaces via `Pick<ZzzRuntime, ...>`
or better yet, narrow `*Deps` interfaces from fuz_app.

modules.module_comment:
Runtime type alias for zzz.

The full runtime interface is shared via `RuntimeDeps` from fuz_app.
`ZzzRuntime` is a project-local alias used throughout the CLI code.

Functions should accept narrow `*Deps` interfaces from
`@fuzdev/fuz_app/runtime/deps.js`, not the full `ZzzRuntime`.
